#585,639 after

#######iPHoP##########

hmp2_vir_iphop <- read.csv("/Users/anshulsinha/Desktop/IBD_prophages_aim/bioinformatics_aim/bioinformatics_HMP_2/virome_data_08_2024/Host_prediction_to_genome_m90.csv", header = TRUE, sep = ",")

length(unique(hmp2_vir_iphop$Virus))
#hmp2_vir_iphop <- read.csv("/Users/anshulsinha/Desktop/IBD_prophages_aim/bioinformatics_aim/bioinformatics_HMP_2/free_virome/free_virome_09_23/iPHoP/Host_prediction_to_genome_m90.csv", header = TRUE, sep = ",")



#Turn this into a data table 

hmp2_vir_iphop <- as.data.table(hmp2_vir_iphop)

hmp2_vir_iphop
#iPhoP gives several outputs for each contig - filter to give the highest confidence 

#If two values have the same confidence score, it'll just take the top value. 

hmp2_vir_iphop <- hmp2_vir_iphop %>%
  group_by(Virus) %>%
  dplyr::slice(which.max(Confidence.score))



colnames(hmp2_vir_iphop)[1] <- c("contigname")




#Separate taxonomic levels 
hmp2_vir_iphop <-  hmp2_vir_iphop %>% 
  separate(Host.taxonomy, into = c("Kingdom", "Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus", "Species"), sep = ";")
hmp2_vir_iphop

names(hmp2_vir_iphop)[1] <- "contigname"
hmp2_vir_iphop


hmp2_vir_iphop <- hmp2_vir_iphop %>%
    mutate(Phylum = recode(Phylum, p__Firmicutes_A = 'p__Firmicutes', p__Firmicutes_B = 'p__Firmicutes', p__Firmicutes_C = 'p__Firmicutes'))
hmp2_vir_iphop





#Read in Bacphlip
  

hmp2_bacphlip <-  read.table("/Users/anshulsinha/Desktop/IBD_prophages_aim/bioinformatics_aim/bioinformatics_HMP_2/virome_data_08_2024/dereplicated_phages.tsv", header = TRUE, sep = "\t")


#Assign Temperate vs. Virulent based on confidence scores 
  
hmp2_bacphlip$Lifestyle <- ifelse(hmp2_bacphlip$Temperate > 0.5, "Temperate", "Virulent")
hmp2_bacphlip
colnames(hmp2_bacphlip)[1] <- c("contigname")

#M2014_k119_126040_flag=0_multi=103.9891_len=376548_fragment_1	0.94950528	0.050494723	

#ALSO - if the contig name contains "fragment" - call it a temperate phage 


library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

hmp2_bacphlip <- hmp2_bacphlip %>%
  mutate(Lifestyle = if_else(str_detect(contigname, "fragment") & Lifestyle == "Virulent", 
                             "Temperate", 
                             Lifestyle))
#test


hmp2_bacphliptest <- hmp2_bacphlip[hmp2_bacphlip$contigname == "C3008_k119_67594_flag=1_multi=21.9943_len=74007_fragment_1",]
hmp2_bacphliptest


table(hmp2_bacphlip$Lifestyle)

df_hmp_virome
df_hmp_temperate
df_hmp_virulent

  group_by(filename) %>%
  summarise(mean_frac = mean(meandepth %% 1))

    summarise(length_normalized_read_count = sum(meandepth))



```

```{r}
#I want to calculate the % rel abundance for temperate phages and add it to the metadata 
#step1 - calculate the mean depth sum for each sample 

library(dplyr)
 df_sum_sample <- sam_tools_cov_hmp2_vir %>%
    group_by(filename) %>%
    summarise(length_normalized_read_count = sum(meandepth))

#step 2 -calcuate the mean depth sum for just the temperate virome and calculate the relab


 df_sum_sample_temp <- df_hmp_temperate %>%
    group_by(filename) %>%
    summarise(temp_length_normalized_read_count = sum(meandepth))

 #step 3- left join the two above 
 
 temp_rel_ab_df <- left_join(df_sum_sample, df_sum_sample_temp, by = "filename")
 temp_rel_ab_df$temp_relab <- temp_rel_ab_df$temp_length_normalized_read_count/temp_rel_ab_df$length_normalized_read_count
temp_rel_ab_df


#step 4 left join back with the metadata 

metadata_df <- left_join(metadata_df, temp_rel_ab_df, by = "filename")


```

```{r}
library(phyloseq)
library(dplyr)

#Creating a function that converts the samtools outputs to a matrix that can be used by Phyloseq 
#option to choose "meandepth" (length normalized read counts) or raw read counts "numreads"

ps_otu_tab_maker <- function(sam_tools_output, reads) {
  
  
  
  #read in and select necessary columns for ps 
  
sam_tools_output_1 <- sam_tools_output %>%
  select(filename, contigname, reads)
sam_tools_output_1




if(reads == "meandepth") {
sam_tools_output_1$meandepth <- round(sam_tools_output_1$meandepth, 0)
}  
  else
{sam_tools_output_1$numreads <- round(sam_tools_output_1$numreads, 0)

}  







#round the decimals - necssary for ps 
#sam_tools_output_1$meandepth <- round(sam_tools_output_1$meandepth, 0)

#sam_tools_output_1


# turn this into a correct format for ps 


sam_tools_output_2 <- sam_tools_output_1 %>%
                 pivot_wider(names_from = filename, values_from = reads)

#replace nas in the df with 0 

sam_tools_output_2[is.na(sam_tools_output_2)] = 0
#sam_tools_output_2

#make the contig column the rownames
 sam_tools_output_3 <- sam_tools_output_2 %>%
  tibble::column_to_rownames("contigname") 
 
# Convert to matrix

sam_tools_matrix <- as.matrix(sam_tools_output_3)
 sam_tools_matrix


}


otu_tab_whole_vir <-  ps_otu_tab_maker(df_hmp_virome, reads = "meandepth")
otu_tab_temp_vir <-ps_otu_tab_maker(df_hmp_temperate, reads = "meandepth")
otu_tab_vir_vir <- ps_otu_tab_maker(df_hmp_virulent, reads = "meandepth")




otu_tab_whole_vir_reads <-  ps_otu_tab_maker(df_hmp_virome, reads = "numreads")




otu_tab_whole_vir
col_to_keep <- "M2039_5_nonIBD"   # if this is a column name

mat_sub <- otu_tab_whole_vir[, col_to_keep, drop = FALSE]

  
test84 <- sam_tools_cov_hmp2_vir[sam_tools_cov_hmp2_vir$filename == "M2039_5_nonIBD",]



as.data.frame(mat_sub)

 filter(!is.na(contigname)) %>%

```{r}

#now work on taxonomy for phyloseq - need the same rownames as the abundance (contigname on the x-axis)

#There are two taxonomy tables I need to create depending on the phyloseq objects I create. Due to limitations in MicroViz, I cant include the IPhoP and BacPhlip Taxonomy info in the same PS object (makes visualization difficult)

#For 3 PS objects (whole virome, temperate, virulent) I'll use the IPhoP taxonomy
# For the 1 PS object where I compare Virulent and Temperate phage rel abundance/diversity, I'll use the bacphlip taxonomy



#IPHoP only includes the contigs with hits, so I need to first include all contigs by making a list of them using the bacphlip df then left join with the iPHoP df


  
hmp2_vir_names <- hmp2_bacphlip %>%
  select(contigname)
hmp2_vir_names

df_hmp_iphop_all_1 <- left_join(hmp2_vir_names, hmp2_vir_iphop, by="contigname")

#also add in the bacphlip info here...  will be useful to include in phyloseq

df_hmp_iphop_all <- left_join(df_hmp_iphop_all_1, hmp2_bacphlip, by="contigname")


table(hmp2_bacphlip$Lifestyle)



# assign first column as rownames
#rownames(df_hmp_free_iphop_all) <- df_hmp_free_iphop_all[, 1]

# remove first column

#df_hmp_free_iphop_all <-  df_hmp_free_iphop_all[, -1, drop = FALSE]


# remove uncessary columns: For IPhop taxa only phyloseq object 

df_hmp_iphop_all_taxa_only <- select(df_hmp_iphop_all, -c("Confidence.score", "Additional.methods", "Virulent", "Temperate", "Lifestyle", "Host.genome"))

df_hmp_iphop_all_taxa_only <- df_hmp_iphop_all_taxa_only %>% 
tibble::column_to_rownames("contigname")


#Breakdown of iPHoP hits 

table(df_hmp_iphop_all_taxa_only$Main.method, useNA = "always")
table(df_hmp_iphop_all_taxa_only$Family, useNA = "always")
df_hmp_iphop_all_taxa_only <- as.matrix(df_hmp_iphop_all_taxa_only)


# remove uncessary columns: For lifestyle info  phyloseq object (see below) - removing all IPhoP taxonomy 



df_hmp_iphop_all_lifestyle <- select(df_hmp_iphop_all, -c("Main.method","Confidence.score", "Additional.methods", "Virulent", "Temperate", "Host.genome", "Kingdom", "Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus", "Species"))

df_hmp_iphop_all_lifestyle <- df_hmp_iphop_all_lifestyle %>% 
tibble::column_to_rownames("contigname")

df_hmp_iphop_all_lifestyle <- as.matrix(df_hmp_iphop_all_lifestyle)






#now for the X phyloseq object - I want to append the phyla names with the lifestyle info (Ie- Proteobacteria_Virulent) #Do this without appendeing the NA's- important for agglomeration later 
df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names  <- df_hmp_iphop_all

df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Kingdom <- ifelse(is.na(df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Kingdom), df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Kingdom, paste0(df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Kingdom, sep = "_", df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Lifestyle))


df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Phylum <- ifelse(is.na(df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Phylum), df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Phylum, paste0(df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Phylum, sep = "_", df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Lifestyle))



df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Class <- ifelse(is.na(df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Class), df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Class, paste0(df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Class, sep = "_", df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Lifestyle))



df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Order <- ifelse(is.na(df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Order), df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Order, paste0(df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Order, sep = "_", df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Lifestyle))



df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Family <- ifelse(is.na(df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Family), df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Family, paste0(df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Family, sep = "_", df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Lifestyle))



df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Genus <- ifelse(is.na(df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Genus), df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Genus, paste0(df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Genus, sep = "_", df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Lifestyle))

df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Species <- ifelse(is.na(df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Species), df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Species, paste0(df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Species, sep = "_", df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Lifestyle))


unique(df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names$Family)


# remove uncessary columns: For IPhop taxa only phyloseq object 

df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names <- select(df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names, -c("Confidence.score", "Additional.methods", "Virulent", "Temperate", "Lifestyle", "Host.genome"))



df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names

df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names <- df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names %>% 
tibble::column_to_rownames("contigname")

df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names <- as.matrix(df_hmp_iphop_all_combined_taxa_names)

```{r}
#metadata make the sample names the rownames
#remove the duplicate samples so that there are the same number of samples as in the samtools cov 
#Ill be able to use the same metadata for all PS objects 



hmp2_samplenames <- sam_tools_cov_hmp2_vir %>%
  select(filename)

length(unique(hmp2_samplenames$filename))
unique_sample_names <-  unique(hmp2_samplenames$filename)
length(unique_sample_names)
unique_sample_names <- as.data.frame(unique_sample_names)
colnames(unique_sample_names) <- "filename"


#now left join
hmp2_metadata_phylo <- left_join(unique_sample_names,metadata_df, by="filename")
hmp2_metadata_phylo

#remove those that don't have dysbiosis scores

hmp2_metadata_phylo <- hmp2_metadata_phylo[complete.cases(hmp2_metadata_phylo$dysbiosis_binary), ]
#Before Creating PS Object, change factor levels 




hmp2_metadata_phylo$dysbiosis_binary <- factor(hmp2_metadata_phylo$dysbiosis_binary, levels=c("No", "Yes"))

hmp2_metadata_phylo$diagnosis.x <- factor(hmp2_metadata_phylo$diagnosis.x, levels=c("nonIBD", "CD", "UC"))


hmp2_metadata_phylo.dup <- hmp2_metadata_phylo[!duplicated(hmp2_metadata_phylo$Participant.ID),]
table(hmp2_metadata_phylo.dup$diagnosis.x)

hist(hmp2_metadata_phylo$length_normalized_read_count)

# Extract sample names
samples <- sample_names(subset_1500)

# Extract metadata as data.frame
meta_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(subset_1500))

# Extract sample_sums (total counts per sample)
sums <- sample_sums(subset_1500)

# Construct your result data frame
result_df <- data.frame(
  sample = samples,
  length_normalized_read_counts = meta_df$length_normalized_read_count,
  sample_sum = sums
)


identical(samples, rownames(meta_df))  # Should be TRUE
identical(samples, names(sums))        # Should be TRUE

# Or verify in the result
all(result_df$sample == rownames(meta_df))  # TRUE
all(result_df$sample == names(sums))        # TRUE


# View side-by-side comparison
cbind(
  sample_names = samples,
  meta_rownames = rownames(meta_df),
  sums_names = names(sums)
)

# Look at first few rows
tail(result_df)
tail(hmp2_metadata_phylo)
meta_df <- data.frame(sample_data(subset_1500))
tail(meta_df)
#MSM5LLIQ_P
meta_df_sub <- meta_df[meta_df$filename == "H4017_37_CD",]


fractional_parts <- df_hmp_temperate_test$meandepth %% 1

# Summarize the distribution
summary(fractional_parts)
mean(fractional_parts)

sample_fractional_means <- df_hmp_virome %>%
  group_by(filename) %>%
  summarise(mean_frac = mean(meandepth %% 1))

#C3001_16_CD_P =C3001_16_CD_P
result_df


sample__means <- df_hmp_virome %>%
  group_by(filename) %>%
    summarise(length_normalized_read_count = sum(meandepth))



sample_means_rounded <- df_hmp_virome %>%
  mutate(meandepth_rounded = round(meandepth, 0)) %>%
  group_by(filename) %>%
  summarise(length_normalized_read_count = sum(meandepth_rounded))



test_sample <- df_hmp_virome %>% 
  filter(filename == "C3001_16_CD_P")  # or whatever the sample name is


unrounded_sum <- sum(test_sample$meandepth)
rounded_sum <- sum(round(test_sample$meandepth, 0))
mean_frac <- mean(test_sample$meandepth %% 1)


data.frame(
  unrounded_sum = unrounded_sum,
  rounded_sum = rounded_sum,
  difference = unrounded_sum - rounded_sum,
  mean_frac = mean_frac
)

test_sample <- df_hmp_virome %>% 
  filter(filename == "C3001_16_CD_P")

# Count how many values are < 0.5 (will round to 0 and be LOST)
test_sample %>%
  summarise(
    n_below_half = sum(meandepth < 0.5),
    sum_below_half = sum(meandepth[meandepth < 0.5]),
    n_total = n()
  )


# Check the 0.5-1.0 range (these round UP to 1)
test_sample %>%
  summarise(
    # Below 0.5: round to 0 (LOSE everything)
    n_below_half = sum(meandepth < 0.5),
    sum_below_half = sum(meandepth[meandepth < 0.5]),
    
    # Between 0.5 and 1.0: round UP to 1 (GAIN)
    n_half_to_one = sum(meandepth >= 0.5 & meandepth < 1.0),
    sum_half_to_one = sum(meandepth[meandepth >= 0.5 & meandepth < 1.0]),
    gain_from_rounding_up = n_half_to_one - sum_half_to_one
  )


sample_range_counts <- df_hmp_virome %>%
  group_by(filename) %>%
  summarise(
    n_below_0.5 = sum(meandepth < 0.5),
    n_0.5_to_1 = sum(meandepth >= 0.5 & meandepth < 1.0),
    
    # Also helpful to see the sums
    sum_below_0.5 = sum(meandepth[meandepth < 0.5]),
    sum_0.5_to_1 = sum(meandepth[meandepth >= 0.5 & meandepth < 1.0]),
    
    # Calculate the net effect
    loss_from_below = sum(meandepth[meandepth < 0.5]),
    gain_from_0.5_to_1 = sum(meandepth >= 0.5 & meandepth < 1.0) - sum(meandepth[meandepth >= 0.5 & meandepth < 1.0]),
    
    net_difference = loss_from_below - gain_from_0.5_to_1
  )

sample_range_analysis <- df_hmp_virome %>%
  group_by(filename) %>%
  summarise(
    # Loss: values < 0.5 become 0
    total_loss = sum(meandepth[meandepth < 0.5]),
    
    # Gain: values 0.5-1.0 become 1
    n_in_range = sum(meandepth >= 0.5 & meandepth < 1.0),
    sum_in_range = sum(meandepth[meandepth >= 0.5 & meandepth < 1.0]),
    total_gain = n_in_range - sum_in_range,  # They become 1 each, so gain is (count - their sum)
    
    # Net effect from JUST these two ranges
    net_from_these_ranges = total_gain - total_loss,
    
    # But also need to account for ALL other rounding
    actual_difference = sum(meandepth) - sum(round(meandepth))
  )

sum(sample_range_analysis$actual_difference < 0)

# This should show net is consistently negative (loss > gain)
summary(sample_range_analysis$net)

#C3002_24_CD

result_df_C <-  result_df[result_df$sample == "C3002_24_CD",]
result_df_C2 <-  result_df[result_df$sample == "C3002_10_CD_P",]


tail(result_df, 50)

test_sample052 <- test_sample1[test_sample1$meandepth > 0.5 & test_sample1$meandepth < 1,]

hmp2_bacphlip_test_temp2 <- hmp2_bacphlip[hmp2_bacphlip$contigname == "P6010_k119_204584_flag=3_multi=42.0009_len=47132",]

hmp2_bacphlip_test_vir2 <- hmp2_bacphlip[hmp2_bacphlip$contigname == "P6028_k119_104589_flag=0_multi=79.7584_len=26577",]

physeqhmp2tempvir
physeqhmp2vir

otu_id_vir2 <- "M2083_k119_23628_flag=1_multi=293.8875_len=52193_fragment_1"

otu_id_vir4 <- "P6028_k119_104589_flag=0_multi=79.7584_len=26577"

otu_id_vir1 %in% taxa_names(physeqhmp2tempvir) #FALSE

otu_id_vir2 %in% taxa_names(physeqhmp2vir) #FALSE
otu_id_vir2 %in% taxa_names(physeqhmp2tempvir) #TRUE

otu_id_vir4 %in% taxa_names(physeqhmp2tempvir) #FALSE

```{r}

#sanity check to ensure sums of  physeqhmp2whole_vir_i is the sum of the two components 


shared_ids <- Reduce(intersect, list(sample_names(physeqhmp2whole_vir_i),
                                     sample_names(physeqhmp2vir),
                                     sample_names(physeqhmp2tempvir)))

length(shared_ids)          

physeq_whole_shared   <- prune_samples(shared_ids, physeqhmp2whole_vir_i)
physeq_vir_shared     <- prune_samples(shared_ids, physeqhmp2vir)
physeq_tempvir_shared <- prune_samples(shared_ids, physeqhmp2tempvir)

ss_whole   <- sample_sums(physeq_whole_shared)
ss_vir     <- sample_sums(physeq_vir_shared)
ss_tempvir <- sample_sums(physeq_tempvir_shared)

# 1) Make sure sample names are identical and aligned

ss_whole   <- sample_sums(physeq_whole_shared)
ss_vir     <- sample_sums(physeq_vir_shared)
ss_tempvir <- sample_sums(physeq_tempvir_shared)

# Reorder vir and tempvir to match whole
ss_vir     <- ss_vir[names(ss_whole)]
ss_tempvir <- ss_tempvir[names(ss_whole)]


all(names(ss_whole) == names(ss_vir))
all(names(ss_whole) == names(ss_tempvir))

table(ss_vir + ss_tempvir == ss_whole)     # should be all TRUE


#method2

# 1) Get sample_sums as small data frames
df_whole <- data.frame(
  sample = names(sample_sums(physeqhmp2whole_vir_i)),
  whole  = as.numeric(sample_sums(physeqhmp2whole_vir_i)),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

df_vir <- data.frame(
  sample = names(sample_sums(physeqhmp2vir)),
  vir    = as.numeric(sample_sums(physeqhmp2vir)),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

df_temp <- data.frame(
  sample = names(sample_sums(physeqhmp2tempvir)),
  temp   = as.numeric(sample_sums(physeqhmp2tempvir)),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
df_joined <- df_whole %>%
  left_join(df_vir,  by = "sample") %>%
  left_join(df_temp, by = "sample")

# Check how many samples have vir/temp data
table(is.na(df_joined$vir), is.na(df_joined$temp))

# Flag samples where both components are present
df_joined <- df_joined %>%
  mutate(
    sum_vir_temp = vir + temp,
    ok= !is.na(sum_vir_temp) & sum_vir_temp == whole
  )

table(df_joined$ok)      # should be TRUE for the 1595 samples


df_joinedna <- df_joined[df_joined$ok == FALSE,]
# Keep only complete cases if desired
df_complete <- df_joined %>%
  filter(!is.na(vir), !is.na(temp))


#sanity

# 1) Subset to the sample of interest
ps_C3001_t <- prune_samples("C3001_14_CD_P", physeqhmp2tempvir)

sample_sums(ps_C3001)
otu_mat <- as(otu_table(ps_C3001), "matrix")
# no transpose

otu_df_C3001_14_CD_P <- as.data.frame(otu_mat)

# Optional: make taxa/contig ID an explicit column
otu_df_C3001_14_CD_P$taxon_id <- rownames(otu_df_C3001_14_CD_P)
otu_df_C3001_14_CD_P$C3001_14_CD_P

otu_df_C3001_14_CD_P_sorted <- otu_df_C3001_14_CD_P[ 
  order(otu_df_C3001_14_CD_P$C3001_14_CD_P, decreasing = TRUE),
]


ps_C3001_v <- prune_samples("C3001_14_CD_P", physeqhmp2vir)
sample_sums(ps_C3001_v)

```{r}
# Sanity check for manuscript: dysbiosis: temperate 
# I calculate it further down in the document but I wanted to sanity check it again - calculating it differently. 
#similar numbers - slightly different prob bc the rarefying changes every time - but overall quite similar/ 





subset_1500.temp_test <-  subset_samples(physeqhmp2tempvir, length_normalized_read_count > 1500)
sample_sums(subset_1500.temp_test)




#tax glom 
subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first_test <- tax_glom(subset_1500.temp_test, taxrank="Family", NArm = TRUE) 


#keep only samples with more than 700 samples - after glomming (and just temperate)
subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first_test_700 <-
  prune_samples(sample_sums(subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first_test) > 700,
                subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first_test)

sample_sums(subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first_test_700)
#1079 samples here


#Rarefy at 700
subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first_test_700_rare <-
  rarefy_even_depth(subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first_test_700,
                    sample.size = 700,
                    replace = FALSE,
                    rngseed = 9,
                    verbose = TRUE)



sample_sums(subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first_test_700_rare)

#Remove non-IBD samples 
ps_no_nonIBD <-
  subset_samples(subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first_test_700_rare,
                 diagnosis.x != "nonIBD")

#755 samples here 

bray_dist_no_non <- phyloseq::distance(ps_no_nonIBD, method = "bray")  # replace with your object name


## 2. Metadata as data.frame
meta_non_non <- as(sample_data(ps_no_nonIBD), "data.frame")

#ensure no NAs

sum(is.na(meta_non_non$dysbiosis_binary))
sum(is.na(meta_non_non$Participant.ID))
table(meta_non_non$diagnosis.x, useNA = "ifany")
table(meta_non_non$diagnosis.y, useNA = "ifany")
table(meta_non_non$dysbiosis_binary, useNA = "ifany")
table(meta_non_non$diagnosis.y, useNA = "ifany")

## 3. Ensure alignment (optional but recommended)
stopifnot(all(rownames(meta_non_non) == attr(bray_dist_no_non, "Labels")))
#h1 <- with(your_data, how(nperm = 999, blocks = subject))
#adonis2(distance ~ treatment, data = your_data, permutations = h1)


## 4. Make sure dysbiosis_binary is a factor
meta_non_non$dysbiosis_binary <- factor(meta_non_non$dysbiosis_binary)
meta_non_non$Participant.ID <- factor(meta_non_non$Participant.ID)


stopifnot(all(rownames(meta_non_non) == attr(bray_dist_no_non, "Labels")))

block_fam_tempCDUC_test <- with(meta_non_non, how(nperm = 999, blocks = Participant.ID))
adonis2(bray_dist_no_non ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta_non_non, permutations = block_fam_tempCDUC_test)
adonis2(bray_dist_no_non ~ dysbiosis_binary + antibiotics, data = meta_non_non, permutations = block_fam_tempCDUC_test, by = "margin")


#


```

```{r}
# Sanity check for manuscript: dysbiosis:virulent

subset_1500.vir_test <-  subset_samples(physeqhmp2vir, length_normalized_read_count > 1500)
2176+1710

#length(intersect(sample_names(subset_1500.vir_test),
   #              sample_names(subset_1500)))


sample_sums(physeqhmp2vir)
#tax glom 
subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first_test_vir <- tax_glom(subset_1500.vir_test, taxrank="Family", NArm = TRUE) 

sample_sums(subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first_test_vir)


#keep only samples with more than 700 samples - after glomming (and just temperate)
subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first_test_700_vir <-
  prune_samples(sample_sums(subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first_test_vir) > 700,
                subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first_test_vir)

#581 samples 


#Rarefy at 700
subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first_test_700_rare_vir <-
  rarefy_even_depth(subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first_test_700_vir,
                    sample.size = 700,
                    replace = FALSE,
                    rngseed = 10,
                    verbose = TRUE)



sample_sums(subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first_test_700_rare_vir)

#Remove non-IBD samples 
ps_no_nonIBD_vir <-
  subset_samples(subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first_test_700_rare_vir,
                 diagnosis.x != "nonIBD")

#381 samples 

bray_dist_no_non_vir <- phyloseq::distance(ps_no_nonIBD_vir, method = "bray")  # replace with your object name
class(bray_dist_no_non_vir)
## 2. Metadata as data.frame
meta_non_non_vir <- as(sample_data(ps_no_nonIBD_vir), "data.frame")

#ensure no NAs

sum(is.na(meta_non_non_vir$dysbiosis_binary))
sum(is.na(meta_non_non_vir$Participant.ID))
table(meta_non_non_vir$diagnosis.x)
table(meta_non_non_vir$diagnosis.y)
table(meta_non_non_vir$dysbiosis_binary)
table(meta_non_non_vir$diagnosis.y)

## 3. Ensure alignment (optional but recommended)
stopifnot(all(rownames(meta_non_non_vir) == attr(bray_dist_no_non_vir, "Labels")))

## 4. Make sure dysbiosis_binary is a factor
meta_non_non_vir$dysbiosis_binary <- factor(meta_non_non_vir$dysbiosis_binary)
meta_non_non_vir$Participant.ID <- factor(meta_non_non_vir$Participant.ID)

block_fam_tempCDUC_test_vir <- with(meta_non_non_vir, how(nperm = 999, blocks = Participant.ID))
adonis2(bray_dist_no_non_vir ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta_non_non_vir, permutations = block_fam_tempCDUC_test_vir)

adonis2(bray_dist_no_non_vir ~ dysbiosis_binary + antibiotics, data = meta_non_non_vir, permutations = block_fam_tempCDUC_test_vir, by ="margin")

#Similar to when I calculate it further down - note that there are slight differences bc

```{r}
#FUNCTION #3

#Ordination PCA using Aitchison's Distance 
#from the microViz tutorial: T
#1) Transform the microbial data with the centered-log-ratio transformation
#2) Perform PCA with the clr-transformed features (equivalent to Aitchison distance PCoA) - use ord_calc()

#Here, the data is normalized "CLR" as per the microviz tutorial
#See discussion on normalization methods in README doc 
# Will also peform a tax aggolomeration at a specified taxonomic level\

```{r}

```



```{r}

library(vegan)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(graphics)
library(cowplot)
###READ COUNT INFO#####
#This is the whole virome ps object - length normalized 
physeqhmp2whole_vir_i
reads_sample <- readcount(physeqhmp2whole_vir_i)

#estimate_richness(physeqhmp2whole_vir_i)

physeqhmp2whole_vir_i

read.counts.df <- as.data.frame(reads_sample)

rownames_to_column(read.counts.df)
colnames(read.counts.df)




ggplot(read.counts.df, aes(x = reads_sample)) + 
    geom_histogram(color = "black", fill = "indianred", binwidth = 350) 

hist(read.counts.df$reads_sample)








## Now lets see if sampling depth is sufficient

#step1 calculate Good's coveage
ps_melt_whole_vir <-  ps_melt(physeqhmp2whole_vir_i) 




 goods.data <- ps_melt_whole_vir %>% 
  group_by(Sample) %>%
  summarise(nseqs= sum(Abundance),
            n_singletons = sum(Abundance == 1), 
            goods = 100*(1- n_singletons/nseqs)) 


  goods.plot <- goods.data %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=nseqs, y=goods)) +
    geom_point()
  

goods.data <- na.omit(goods.data)

mean(goods.data$goods)

#step2 rarefaction curve  

mat <- as(t(otu_table(physeqhmp2whole_vir_i)), "matrix")
raremax <- min(rowSums(mat))



random_rows <- sample(nrow(mat), 50)
matrix.sub <- mat[random_rows, ]


subset_mat.pre.filt <- mat[1:50, ]

rarecurve(subset_mat.pre.filt, step = 20, sample = raremax, col = "coral", cex = 0.6, label = FALSE, main="Before Filtering")



median(goods.data$goods)

# filter samples that have less than 1500 counts 


subset_1500 <-  subset_samples(physeqhmp2whole_vir_i, length_normalized_read_count > 1500)

sort(sample_sums(subset_1500))


mat.1500 <- as(t(otu_table(subset_1500)), "matrix")

#estimate_richness(subset_1500)


random_rows <- sample(nrow(mat.1500), 50)
matrix.sub <- mat.1500[random_rows, ]


#raremax <- min(rowSums(mat))




rarecurve(matrix.sub, step = 20, col = "coral", cex = 0.6, label = FALSE, main = "After Filtering") 

#combine 



#calculate Good's for filtered PS

ps_melt_whole_vir.1500 <-  ps_melt(subset_1500) 


 goods.data.1500 <- ps_melt_whole_vir.1500 %>% 
  group_by(Sample) %>%
  summarise(nseqs= sum(Abundance),
            n_singletons = sum(Abundance == 1), 
            goods = 100*(1- n_singletons/nseqs)) 
  
   
   
  goods.plot.1500 <- goods.data.1500 %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=nseqs, y=goods)) +
    geom_point() + 
    ylim(0,100)
  
  goods.data.1500 <- na.omit(goods.data.1500)

mean(goods.data.1500$goods)
median(goods.data.1500$goods)


#cowplot both good's 


  goods.plot <- goods.data %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=nseqs, y=goods)) +
    geom_point() + 
        xlab("Number of Length-Normalized Reads") + 
        ylab("Good's Coverage") +
        ggtitle("Before Filtering")

  
  goods.plot.1500 <- goods.data.1500 %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=nseqs, y=goods)) +
    geom_point() + 
    ylim(0,100) + 
        xlab("Number of Length-Normalized Reads") + 
        ylab("Good's Coverage") + 
        ggtitle("After Filtering")

    
    
  


goods_plot_combined <- plot_grid(goods.plot, goods.plot.1500)




#COMPARE BREAKDOWN OF SAMPLES BEFORE/AFTER
#What is breakdown of diagnosis/dysbiosis? 

# Count the number of rows per diagnosis
whole.vir.sampledata <-  data.frame(sample_data(physeqhmp2whole_vir_i))



# Count the number of rows per diagnosis
diagnosis_counts <- whole.vir.sampledata %>%
  dplyr::count(diagnosis.x)
  
  

# Calculate the percentage for labels
diagnosis_counts <- diagnosis_counts %>%
  mutate(percentage = n / sum(n) * 100,
         label = paste0(diagnosis.x, ": ", n, " (", round(percentage, 1), "%)"))

# Create the pie chart with labels
diag_pie <- ggplot(diagnosis_counts, aes(x = "", y = n, fill = diagnosis.x)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  labs(fill = "Diagnosis", y = "Count", x = "") +
  theme_void() +  # Removes axes and background
  theme(legend.position = "right") +
  geom_text(aes(label = label), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 3) + 
  ggtitle("Before Filtering")


# Count the number of rows per dysbiosis
dybsiosis_counts <- whole.vir.sampledata %>%
  dplyr::count(dysbiosis_binary)

# Calculate the percentage for labels
dybsiosis_counts <- dybsiosis_counts %>%
  mutate(percentage = n / sum(n) * 100,
         label = paste0(dysbiosis_binary, ": ", n, " (", round(percentage, 1), "%)"))

# Create the pie chart with labels
dysb_pie <- ggplot(dybsiosis_counts, aes(x = "", y = n, fill = dysbiosis_binary)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  labs(fill = "Dysbiosis", y = "Count", x = "") +
  theme_void() +  # Removes axes and background
  theme(legend.position = "right") +
  geom_text(aes(label = label), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 3) + 
  ggtitle("Before Filtering")





#number of reads between groups 
read.counts.df.merged <- read.counts.df %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "filename") 

read.counts.df.merged <- left_join(read.counts.df.merged,hmp2_metadata_phylo, by="filename")


#Compare disease status vs. Diagnosis Status 

ggplot(read.counts.df.merged, aes(x=diagnosis.x, y=reads_sample, fill = diagnosis.x))+
  geom_boxplot() + 
  theme_minimal()



#disease 
kruskal.test(reads_sample ~ diagnosis.x, data = read.counts.df.merged)
pairwise.wilcox.test(read.counts.df.merged$reads_sample, read.counts.df.merged$diagnosis.x,
                 p.adjust.method = "BH")



#dysbiosis 

ggplot(read.counts.df.merged, aes(x=dysbiosis_binary, y=reads_sample, fill = dysbiosis_binary))+
  geom_boxplot() + 
  theme_minimal()


wilcox.test(reads_sample ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = read.counts.df.merged)



####BEFORE#####
filtered.sampledata <-  data.frame(sample_data(physeqhmp2whole_vir_i))

unique.sampledata <- filtered.sampledata[!duplicated(filtered.sampledata$Participant.ID),]


length(unique(unique.sampledata$Participant.ID)) #130 individuals before filtering 

table(unique.sampledata$diagnosis.x) #27 non IBD, 65 CD, 38 UC before filtering 





####AFTER####

filtered.1500.sampledata <-  data.frame(sample_data(subset_1500))


filtered.1500.taxtab <- data.frame(tax_table(subset_1500))
#get unique number of individuals 

length(unique(filtered.1500.sampledata$Participant.ID)) #115 samples after filtering 

unique.1500 <- filtered.1500.sampledata[!duplicated(filtered.1500.sampledata$Participant.ID),]

table(unique.1500$diagnosis.x) #27 non IBD, 57 CD, 31 UC after filtering 



length(unique(filtered.1500.sampledata$filename)) #1093 samples after filtering 
length(unique(read.counts.df$filename)) #1595 samples before






#COMPARE BREAKDOWN OF SAMPLES BEFORE/AFTER
#What is breakdown of diagnosis/dysbiosis? 
# Count the number of rows per diagnosis


diagnosis_counts.1500 <- filtered.1500.sampledata %>%
  dplyr::count(diagnosis.x)


# Calculate the percentage for labels
diagnosis_counts.1500 <- diagnosis_counts.1500 %>%
  mutate(percentage = n / sum(n) * 100,
         label = paste0(diagnosis.x, ": ", n, " (", round(percentage, 1), "%)"))


# Create the pie chart with labels
diag_pie_after <- ggplot(diagnosis_counts.1500, aes(x = "", y = n, fill = diagnosis.x)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  labs(fill = "Diagnosis", y = "Count", x = "") +
  theme_void() +  # Removes axes and background
  theme(legend.position = "right") +
   geom_text(aes(label = label), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 3) + 
  ggtitle("After Filtering")

# Count the number of rows per dysbi
dysbiosis.counts.1500 <- filtered.1500.sampledata %>%
  dplyr::count(dysbiosis_binary)


# Calculate the percentage for labels
dysbiosis.counts.1500 <- dysbiosis.counts.1500 %>%
  mutate(percentage = n / sum(n) * 100,
         label = paste0(dysbiosis_binary, ": ", n, " (", round(percentage, 1), "%)"))


# Create the pie chart with labels
dysb_pie_after <-  ggplot(dysbiosis.counts.1500, aes(x = "", y = n, fill = dysbiosis_binary)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  labs(fill = "Dysbiosis", y = "Count", x = "") +
  theme_void() +  # Removes axes and background
  theme(legend.position = "right") +
   geom_text(aes(label = label), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 3) + 
  ggtitle("After Filtering")

pie_diag_plot_combined <- plot_grid(diag_pie, diag_pie_after)
pie_diag_plot_combined
pie_dysb_plot_combined <- plot_grid(dysb_pie, dysb_pie_after, align = "v")
pie_dysb_plot_combined

all_combined <- plot_grid(diag_pie, diag_pie_after,dysb_pie, dysb_pie_after, align = "v")
```
```{r}
#Recreate PHF observations with different code: 

subset_1500
library(phyloseq)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

rarefied.reads.1500 <-  rarefy_even_depth(subset_1500, sample.size = min(sample_sums(subset_1500)),
  rngseed = 1, replace = TRUE, trimOTUs = TRUE, verbose = TRUE)


# ps: your phyloseq object
# assume sample_data(ps)$Participant.ID holds the individual ID

bray_disttest <- phyloseq::distance(rarefied.reads.1500, method = "bray")

metatest <- as(sample_data(rarefied.reads.1500), "data.frame")
metatest$filename <- rownames(metatest)

# sanity: check ordering
stopifnot(all(rownames(metatest) == attr(bray_disttest, "Labels")))

bray_mat <- as.matrix(bray_disttest)

bray_df <- bray_mat %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("sample1") %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -sample1,
    names_to = "sample2",
    values_to = "bray"
  ) %>%
  # drop self-comparisons
  filter(sample1 != sample2) %>%
  # attach subject IDs
  left_join(metatest %>% select(sample1 = filename, subject1 = Participant.ID),
            by = "sample1") %>%
  left_join(metatest %>% select(sample2 = filename, subject2 = Participant.ID),
            by = "sample2") %>%
  mutate(
    same_subject = subject1 == subject2,
    same_subject = ifelse(same_subject, "Intra", "Inter")
  )

#1,193,556 rows

#Should be in a 1093 sample matrix how many bray comparisons (not including self should I have)
#should be 596,778
# 596,778


bray_df <- bray_df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(sorted_pair = ifelse(sample1 < sample2,
                              paste(sample1, sample2, sep = "_"),
                              paste(sample2, sample1, sep = "_"))) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(sorted_pair) %>%
  dplyr::slice(1) %>%
  ungroup()



#Sanity check to compare values in the df 

test_pair <- bray_df %>% filter(sample1 == "C3023_38_CD" & sample2 == "H4006_46_CD")
#0.9532495


bray_from_matrix <- bray_mat["C3023_38_CD", "H4006_46_CD"] # 0.9532495


ggplot(bray_df, aes(x = same_subject, y = bray)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  xlab("Pair type") +
  ylab("Bray-Curtis dissimilarity")


bray_summary <- bray_df %>%
  group_by(same_subject) %>%        # "Intra" vs "Inter"
  summarise(
    n_pairs = n(),
    mean_bray   = mean(bray, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_bray = median(bray, na.rm = TRUE)
  )





#sanity check to see if values are aligned: 0.5341678 between the below two samples in the bray_df 



#and 
bray_mat["C3001_12_CD_P", "C3001_38_CD"] #0.5341678
bray_mat["C3001_38_CD", "C3001_12_CD_P"]   #

bray_mat["C3015_40_UC", "C3015_42_UC"]   
bray_mat["C3015_40_UC", "C3016_36_CD"]   

bray_df
bray_summary




library(phyloseq)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)

# 1. Agglomerate to Family
subset_1500_fam <- tax_glom(rarefied.reads.1500, taxrank = "Family", NArm = TRUE)

sample_sums(subset_1500_fam)
# 2. Bray–Curtis distance at Family level
bray_dist_fam <- phyloseq::distance(subset_1500_fam, method = "bray")

metatest_fam <- as(sample_data(subset_1500_fam), "data.frame")
metatest_fam$filename <- rownames(metatest_fam)

# sanity: check ordering
stopifnot(all(rownames(metatest_fam) == attr(bray_dist_fam, "Labels")))

bray_mat_fam <- as.matrix(bray_dist_fam)

# 3. Long-format with intra/inter labels
bray_df_fam <- bray_mat_fam %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("sample1") %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -sample1,
    names_to = "sample2",
    values_to = "bray"
  ) %>%
  filter(sample1 != sample2) %>%
  left_join(
    metatest_fam %>% select(sample1 = filename, subject1 = Participant.ID),
    by = "sample1"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    metatest_fam %>% select(sample2 = filename, subject2 = Participant.ID),
    by = "sample2"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    same_subject = ifelse(subject1 == subject2, "Intra", "Inter")
  )


#1,193,556 rows

# 4. Collapse A–B vs B–A
bray_df_fam <- bray_df_fam %>%
  dplyr::mutate(sorted_pair = ifelse(sample1 < sample2,
                              paste(sample1, sample2, sep = "_"),
                              paste(sample2, sample1, sep = "_"))) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(sorted_pair) %>%
  dplyr::slice(1) %>%
  ungroup()


#1,193,556 rows



bray_summaryfam <- bray_df_fam %>%
  group_by(same_subject) %>%        # "Intra" vs "Inter"
  summarise(
    n_pairs = n(),
    mean_bray   = mean(bray, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_bray = median(bray, na.rm = TRUE)
  )


#596,778 rows 

test_pair2 <- bray_df_fam %>% filter(sample1 == "C3001_18_CD_P" & sample2 == "H4022_10_nonIBD")
#0.6744186


bray_from_matrix2 <- bray_mat_fam["C3023_38_CD", "H4006_46_CD"] # 0.6744186


bray_summary

ggplot(bray_df_fam, aes(x = same_subject, y = bray)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  xlab("Pair type") +
  ylab("Bray-Curtis dissimilarity")


bray_df_votu <- bray_df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(metric = "vOTU") %>%
  select(sorted_pair, sample1, sample2, same_subject, bray, metric)

bray_df_fam2 <- bray_df_fam %>%
  dplyr::mutate(metric = "Family") %>%
  select(sorted_pair, sample1, sample2, same_subject, bray, metric)

bray_all <- bind_rows(bray_df_votu, bray_df_fam2)



ggplot(bray_all, aes(x = same_subject, y = bray, fill = metric)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  #geom_jitter(alpha = 0.1, size = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#C3B1E1", "#a0d6b4")) +
  labs(x = "Pair Type", y = "Bray-Curtis Dissimilarity", fill = "Metric") +
  theme_bw()




bray_all_test_intra <- bray_all[bray_all$same_subject == "Intra",]
bray_all_test_inter <- bray_all[bray_all$same_subject == "Inter",]

 bray_all_test_inter
bray_all_test_intra
intra <- bray_all %>%
  filter(same_subject == "Intra") %>%
  select(sorted_pair, metric, bray) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = metric, values_from = bray)

wilcox.test(intra$vOTU, intra$Family, paired = TRUE)

bray_df  
bray_df_fam

bray_df #C3001_12_CD_P	C3001_16_CD_P = 0.5742373
bray_df_fam #0.2258065

#0.5742373	0.22580645

  filter(same_subject == "Inter") %>%

  group_by(Sample) %>%
  summarise(nseqs= sum(Abundance),
            n_singletons = sum(Abundance == 1), 
            goods = 100*(1- n_singletons/nseqs))

```{r}

#Before creating functions to calculate distance and plot PCoAs, let's do a sanity check 

#To test that glomming works as expected



ps1.test <- tax_glom(subset_1500, taxrank="Family", NArm = TRUE) 
ps1.test.2 <- tax_glom(subset_1500, taxrank="Family", NArm = FALSE) 
sum(is.na(tax_table(subset_1500)[,"Family"]))
ntaxa(tax_glom(subset_1500, taxrank="Family", NArm=FALSE))
ntaxa(tax_glom(subset_1500, taxrank="Family", NArm=TRUE))

subset_1500 #3,886 taxa if not tax glomming
ps1.test #74 if I tax glom and remove those that don't have family designations 
ps1.test.2



ps.melt.1500 <- 
unique(tax_table(subset_1500)[,"Family"])


tax_glom(subset_1500, taxrank="Family", NArm = TRUE) 



#check counts 
# Run tax_glom to aggregate by 'Family'
ps_family_test  <- tax_glom(subset_1500, taxrank = "Family",  NArm = TRUE)

# Pick a sample and a family to check
sample_to_check <- sample_names(ps_family_test)[52]
family_to_check <- "f__Bacteroidaceae" # Replace with a relevant family name

# Get OTU table as matrix for pre-glom
otu_pre <- as.matrix(otu_table(subset_1500))
tax_table_pre <- as(tax_table(subset_1500), "matrix")


# Ensure taxa are rows
if (!taxa_are_rows(subset_1500)) {
  otu_pre <- t(otu_pre)
}
species_in_family <- rownames(tax_table_pre)[
  !is.na(tax_table_pre[, "Family"]) &
    tax_table_pre[, "Family"] == family_to_check
]

length(species_in_family)


num_species_nonzero <- sum(
  otu_pre[species_in_family, sample_to_check] > 0
)

pre_glom_sum <- sum(
  otu_pre[species_in_family, sample_to_check],
  na.rm = TRUE
)

cat("Number of species with >0 hits:", num_species_nonzero, "\n")
cat("Pre-glom sum:", pre_glom_sum, "\n")

# Find all species/OTUs in the specified familyfamily_row_post <- which(tax_post[, "Family"] == family_to_check)

# Sanity check
otu_post <- as(otu_table(ps_family_test), "matrix")
tax_post <- as(tax_table(ps_family_test), "matrix")

if (!taxa_are_rows(ps_family_test)) {
  otu_post <- t(otu_post)
  
  
}
family_row_post <- which(tax_post[, "Family"] == family_to_check)

# Sanity check
stopifnot(length(family_row_post) == 1)

post_glom_sum <- otu_post[family_row_post, sample_to_check]

cat("Post-glom sum:", post_glom_sum, "\n")

```{r}
###READ COUNT INFO##### #FAMILY LEVEL INFO - use filtered PS 


phf_fam_whole_virome <- tax_glom(subset_1500, taxrank="Family", NArm = TRUE) 


reads_sample_fam <- readcount(phf_fam_whole_virome)





read.counts.df.fam <- as.data.frame(reads_sample_fam)

rownames_to_column(read.counts.df.fam)
colnames(read.counts.df.fam)





ggplot(read.counts.df.fam, aes(x = reads_sample_fam)) + 
    geom_histogram(color = "black", fill = "indianred", binwidth = 350) 

hist(read.counts.df$reads_sample)



## Now lets see if sampling depth is sufficient

#step1 calculate Good's coveage

ps_melt_whole_vir_fam <-  ps_melt(phf_fam_whole_virome) 




 goods.data.fam <- ps_melt_whole_vir_fam %>% 
  group_by(Sample) %>%
  summarise(nseqs= sum(Abundance),
            n_singletons = sum(Abundance == 1), 
            goods = 100*(1- n_singletons/nseqs)) 

goods.plot.fam <- goods.data.fam %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = nseqs, y = goods)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100))

goods.data.fam <- na.omit(goods.data.fam)
goods.data.fam
goods.plot.fam
mean(goods.data.fam$goods)
median(goods.data.fam$goods)

#step2 rarefaction curve  

mat.fam <- as(t(otu_table(phf_fam_whole_virome)), "matrix")
raremax.fam <- min(rowSums(mat.fam))



random_rows.fam <- sample(nrow(mat.fam), 50)
matrix.sub.fam <- mat.fam[random_rows.fam, ]


subset_mat.pre.filt.fam <- matrix.sub.fam[1:50, ]

rarecurve(subset_mat.pre.filt.fam, step = 20, col = "coral", cex = 0.6, label = FALSE, main="Family Level")

### SANITY CHECK TO COMPARE OG READ COUNTS, FAMILY READ COUNTS AND TEMP FAM
#should be loss between OG and family (removing NA family) and from family to temp family



reads_sample_fam_temp <- readcount(phf_fam_temp_virome)
min(reads_sample_fam_temp)

read.counts.df.fam.temp <- as.data.frame(reads_sample_fam_temp)


read.counts.df.fam <- read.counts.df.fam %>% tibble::rownames_to_column("id")
read.counts.df.fam.temp <- read.counts.df.fam.temp %>% tibble::rownames_to_column("id")
read.counts.df.2  <- read.counts.df %>% tibble::rownames_to_column("id")
joined_df <- full_join(read.counts.df.fam, read.counts.df.fam.temp, by = "id")
joined_df2 <- full_join(joined_df, read.counts.df.2, by = "id")




richness1 <- estimate_richness(phf_fam_temp_virome, measures = c("Observed", "Chao1", "Shannon"))
richness2 <- estimate_richness(phf_fam_whole_virome, measures = c("Observed", "Chao1", "Shannon"))
richness1
richness2

 goods.data.fam.temp <- ps_melt_temp_fam %>% 
  group_by(Sample) %>%

rarecurve(subset_mat.pre.filt.fam.vir, step = 20, col = "coral", cex = 0.6, label = FALSE, main="Family Level")


rarecurve(
  subset_mat.pre.filt.fam.vir,
  step = 200,
  col = "coral",
  cex = 0.6,
  label = FALSE,
  main = "Family Level",
  xlim = c(0, 700)
)

#this will tell us how many iphop predictions are in the filtered samples 
#get names of those samples 

filtered.1500.sampledata <-  data.frame(sample_data(subset_1500))

sample.names.1500 <- filtered.1500.sampledata$filename

#filter df_hmp_virome for those counts 
filtered.1500.df_hmp_virome <- df_hmp_virome %>%
  filter(filename %in% sample.names.1500)

df_hmp_virome_merged.2 <- left_join(filtered.1500.df_hmp_virome, hmp2_vir_iphop, by="contigname")

length(unique(df_hmp_virome_merged.2$contigname)) ##3,870 vOTUs in those samples!!!!

#what percentage have a Family level prediction





df_hmp_virome_merged.2.not.na <- df_hmp_virome_merged.2[!is.na(df_hmp_virome_merged.2$Family),]
length(unique(df_hmp_virome_merged.2.not.na$contigname)) ##3,370 vOTUs in those samples!!!!



3370/3870 
#what percentage of contigs have a prediction. - 87.08% = 87.1%

#how many PHFs
length(unique(df_hmp_virome_merged.2.not.na$Family)) ##74







######
df_unique <- df_hmp_virome_merged.2 %>% distinct(contigname, .keep_all = TRUE)

df_unique.2 <- df_unique %>% mutate_all(~replace(., is.na(.), "unclassified"))

df_unique.2.counts <- df_unique.2 %>% 
  group_by(Family) %>% 
  summarize(count=n_distinct(contigname))


# Summarize the data
df_unique.2.counts.summary <- df_unique.2.counts %>%
  group_by(Family) %>%
  summarize(TotalCount = sum(count)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(desc(TotalCount))

top_families <- df_unique.2.counts.summary %>%
  top_n(11, TotalCount) %>%
  mutate(Family = as.character(Family))



other_count <- df_unique.2.counts.summary %>%
  filter(!Family %in% top_families$Family) %>%
  summarize(Family = "Other", TotalCount = sum(TotalCount))


final_data <- bind_rows(top_families, other_count)

final_data$totalsum <- sum(final_data$TotalCount)

final_data$proportion <- final_data$TotalCount/final_data$totalsum 




ggplot(final_data, aes(x = "", y = proportion, fill = Family)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(y = "Value", fill = "Subcategory") +
scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(12, "Set3")) +
  labs(y = "Relative Proportion", x = "PHF", fill = "KEGG Metabolism Category") +
  theme_minimal() + 
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8,angle = 45, hjust = 1, face="italic")) + 
  
  theme(
    legend.key.size = unit(0.2, "cm"),  # Key size
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9)  # Text size
  ) + 
  theme(legend.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5))

```{r}
#now rarefy and perform distance calculations 
#look at distance 



rarefied.reads.1500 <-  rarefy_even_depth(subset_1500, sample.size = min(sample_sums(subset_1500)),
  rngseed = 1, replace = TRUE, trimOTUs = TRUE, verbose = TRUE)


#Make a function that creates a PcoA plot at the vOTU level 
#Take a phyloseq object and distance matrix and the groups for comparison 
###USE A RAREFIED PS OBJECT IF USING BRAY 
#### USE A NON-RAREFIED PS IF USING AITCH



PCoA_vOTU  <- function(ps, distance, group_var) {

#can directly calculate bray distance from here since we have rarefied 
ps.fixed <- ps %>%
 tax_fix(unknowns = "NA")
  

dist.ps.fixed <- ps.fixed  %>% 
  dist_calc(distance)
    
dist.ps.fixed.ord <- dist.ps.fixed  %>% 
  ord_calc("PCoA") 

 ord_plot(dist.ps.fixed.ord, color = group_var, size = 2, interactive = FALSE) + 
   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Dark2")
 
  
}


#Make a function that creates a PcoA plot at the vOTU level 
#Take a phyloseq object and distance matrix and the groups for comparison 
###USE A RAREFIED PS OBJECT IF USING BRAY 
#### USE A NON-RAREFIED PS IF USING AITCH
PCoA_PBF  <- function(ps, distance, group_var) {


  
ps1 <- tax_glom(ps, taxrank="Family", NArm = TRUE) 

dist.ps.fixed <- ps1 %>%
 tax_fix(unknowns = "NA") %>%
  dist_calc(distance)
  

  

fixed.ps.dist.ord <- dist.ps.fixed  %>% 
  ord_calc("PCoA")
 
 ord_plot(fixed.ps.dist.ord, color = group_var, size = 2, interactive = FALSE) + 
   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Dark2")
 
 
}


#use rarefied if using bray, non-rarefied if Aitch
vOTU_perm  <- function(ps, distance, group_var) {

#can directly calculate bray distance from here since we have rarefied 
ps.fixed <- ps %>%
 tax_fix(unknowns = "NA")
  

dist.ps.fixed <- ps.fixed  %>% 
  dist_calc(distance)
    
  perm <- dist.ps.fixed %>%
  dist_permanova(
    seed = 1234, # for set.seed to ensure reproducibility of random process
    n_processes = 1, n_perms = 999, # you should use at least 999!
    variables = group_var
  )
 
 
   perm_get(perm) %>% as.data.frame()

 
  
}


PBF_perm  <- function(ps, distance, group_var) {

#can directly calculate bray distance from here since we have rarefied 


    
ps1 <- tax_glom(ps, taxrank="Family", NArm = TRUE) 

dist.ps.fixed <- ps1 %>%
 tax_fix(unknowns = "NA") %>%
  dist_calc(distance)
  
    
  perm <- dist.ps.fixed %>%
  dist_permanova(
    seed = 1234, # for set.seed to ensure reproducibility of random process
    n_processes = 1, n_perms = 999, # you should use at least 999!
    variables = group_var
  )
 
 
   perm_get(perm) %>% as.data.frame()

 
  
}



#vOTU - Bray - Using rarefied DF 
PCoA_vOTU.Bray.Diagnosis <-   PCoA_vOTU(rarefied.reads.1500, "bray", "diagnosis.x")
PCoA_vOTU.Bray.Dysbiosis <-   PCoA_vOTU(rarefied.reads.1500, "bray", "dysbiosis_binary")



#vOTU - Aitchison's - Using non-rarefied DF 


PCoA_vOTU.Aitch.Diagnosis <-   PCoA_vOTU(subset_1500, "aitchison", "diagnosis.x")
PCoA_vOTU.Aitch.Dysbiosis <-   PCoA_vOTU(subset_1500, "aitchison", "dysbiosis_binary")





#PBF - Bray - Using rarefied DF 
PCoA_PBF.Bray.Diagnosis <-   PCoA_PBF(rarefied.reads.1500, "bray", "diagnosis.x")
PCoA_PBF.Bray.Dysbiosis <-   PCoA_PBF(rarefied.reads.1500, "bray", "dysbiosis_binary")







#PBF -  - Using rarefied DF 
PCoA_PBF.Aitchison.Diagnosis <-   PCoA_PBF(subset_1500, "aitchison", "diagnosis.x")
PCoA_PBF.Aitchison.Dysbiosis <-   PCoA_PBF(subset_1500, "aitchison", "dysbiosis_binary")

PCoA_vOTU.Bray.Dysbiosis
PCoA_PBF.Bray.Dysbiosis

#PBF_perm(rarefied.reads.1500, "bray", "dysbiosis_binary")

PCoA_vOTU.Bray.Diagnosis
PCoA_PBF.Bray.Diagnosis

PCoA_vOTU.Aitch.Dysbiosis
PCoA_PBF.Aitchison.Dysbiosis
vOTU_perm(subset_1500, "aitchison", "dysbiosis_binary")

```{r}
#sanity check - length normalized count PCoA
rarefied.reads.1500


# Bray–Curtis distance
bray.sanity.1500.rar <- distance(rarefied.reads.1500, method = "bray")

ord.sanity.1500.rar <- ordinate(rarefied.reads.1500, method = "PCoA", distance = bray.sanity.1500.rar)


sample_data(rarefied.reads.1500)
# Plot with phyloseq's built-in plot_ordination
plot_ordination(rarefied.reads.1500, ord.sanity.1500.rar, color = "length_normalized_read_count") +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_viridis_c()


meta.sanity <- as(sample_data(rarefied.reads.1500), "data.frame")

set.seed(123)
adonis2(bray.sanity.1500.rar ~ length_normalized_read_count, data = meta.sanity, permutations = 999)
adonis2(bray.sanity.1500.rar ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta.sanity, permutations = 999)

#now do the same but make length_normalized_count categorical
rarefied.reads.1500.2 <- rarefied.reads.1500
# 1. Extract metadata from your phyloseq object
meta.sanity2 <- as.data.frame(sample_data(rarefied.reads.1500.2))

# 2. Create a categorical version of 'length_normalized_read_count'
meta.sanity2$DepthCategory <- ifelse(meta.sanity2$length_normalized_read_count < 2500, "low", "high")

# (Optional: convert to factor so ordering is consistent)
meta.sanity2$DepthCategory <- factor(meta.sanity2$DepthCategory, levels = c("low", "high"))

# 3. Replace the sample_data in your phyloseq object
sample_data(rarefied.reads.1500.2)$DepthCategory <- meta.sanity2$DepthCategory

# 4. Reuse your previous ordination
bray.sanity.1500.rar.2 <- phyloseq::distance(rarefied.reads.1500.2, method = "bray")
ord2.sanity.1500.rar.2 <- ordinate(rarefied.reads.1500.2, method = "PCoA", distance = bray.sanity.1500.rar.2)

# 5. Plot PCoA colored by the new categorical variable
plot_ordination(rarefied.reads.1500.2, ord2, color = "DepthCategory") +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "C") +
  labs(color = "Length-normalized\nread count category",
       x = "PCoA1",
       y = "PCoA2")

plot_ordination(rarefied.reads.1500.2, ord2, color = "dysbiosis_binary") +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "C") +
  labs(color = "dysbiosis_binary",
       x = "PCoA1",
       y = "PCoA2")
```


```{r}
#same but at family level: 

family.sanity <- tax_glom(rarefied.reads.1500, taxrank="Family", NArm = TRUE) 
# 1. Extract metadata from your phyloseq object
meta.fam.sanity <- as.data.frame(sample_data(family.sanity))

# 2. Create a categorical version of 'length_normalized_read_count'
meta.fam.sanity$DepthCategory <- ifelse(meta.fam.sanity$length_normalized_read_count < 2500, "low", "high")

# (Optional: convert to factor so ordering is consistent)
meta.fam.sanity$DepthCategory <- factor(meta.fam.sanity$DepthCategory, levels = c("low", "high"))

# 3. Replace the sample_data in your phyloseq object
sample_data(family.sanity)$DepthCategory <- meta.fam.sanity$DepthCategory


identical(rownames(meta.fam.sanity), sample_names(family.sanity))

# 4. Reuse your previous ordination
bray.sanity.fam.3 <- phyloseq::distance(family.sanity, method = "bray")


as.matrix(bray.sanity.fam.3)
ord3.sanity.fam.3 <- ordinate(family.sanity, method = "PCoA", distance = bray.sanity.fam.3)

# 5. Plot PCoA colored by the new categorical variable
plot_ordination(family.sanity, ord3.sanity.fam.3, color = "DepthCategory") +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "C") +
  labs(color = "Length-normalized\nread count category",
       x = "PCoA1",
       y = "PCoA2")

plot_ordination(family.sanity, ord3.sanity.fam.3, color = "dysbiosis_binary") +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "C") +
  labs(color = "dysbiosis_binary",
       x = "PCoA1",
       y = "PCoA2")

meta.fam.sanity <- as(sample_data(family.sanity), "data.frame")
identical(rownames(meta.fam.sanity), sample_names(family.sanity))

adonis2(bray.sanity.fam.3 ~ length_normalized_read_count, data = meta.fam.sanity, permutations = 999)
adonis2(bray.sanity.fam.3 ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta.fam.sanity, permutations = 999)



#Now for family level - see if Good's coverage makes an impact 

#1 make my sample column rownames 
goods.data.fam.2 <- as.data.frame(goods.data.fam)
rownames(goods.data.fam.2) <- goods.data.fam.2$Sample
goods.data.fam.2$Sample <- NULL  # optional: remove first column


#2 check sample names align 

all(rownames(goods.data.fam.2) %in% sample_names(family.sanity))
identical(rownames(goods.data.fam.2), sample_names(family.sanity))



# Reorder the dataframe to match phyloseq sample order
goods.data.fam.2 <- goods.data.fam.2[sample_names(family.sanity), , drop = FALSE]
identical(rownames(goods.data.fam.2), sample_names(family.sanity))

# Then add the column
sample_data(family.sanity)$goods <- goods.data.fam.2$goods
meta.fam.sanity.2 <- as(sample_data(family.sanity), "data.frame")

adonis2(bray.sanity.fam.3 ~ goods, data = meta.fam.sanity.2, permutations = 999)




# 4. Reuse your previous ordination
bray.sanity.fam.4 <- phyloseq::distance(family.sanity, method = "bray")
as.matrix(bray.sanity.fam.4)
ord3.sanity.fam.4 <- ordinate(family.sanity, method = "PCoA", distance = bray.sanity.fam.4)

# 5. Plot PCoA colored by the new categorical variable
plot_ordination(family.sanity, ord3.sanity.fam.4, color = "goods") +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "C") +
  labs(color = "Goods",
       x = "PCoA1",
       y = "PCoA2")

```{r}
#sanity check does order of rarefy and glom change anything - 

subset_1500



# Rarefy at depth = 1500
ps_rarefied_first <- rarefy_even_depth(subset_1500,
                                       sample.size = 1500,
                                       rngseed = 1,
                                       replace = FALSE,
                                       verbose = FALSE)

# Agglomerate at Family level
ps_rare_glom <- tax_glom(ps_rarefied_first, taxrank = "Family", NArm=TRUE)

# Distance matrix
dist_rare_glom <- distance(ps_rare_glom, method = "bray")

# Adonis2
adonis_rare_glom <- adonis2(dist_rare_glom ~ dysbiosis_binary,
                            data = as(sample_data(ps_rare_glom), "data.frame"))
adonis_rare_glom




# Agglomerate first
ps_glom_first <- tax_glom(subset_1500, taxrank = "Family", NArm=TRUE)

# Rarefy after
ps_glom_rarefied <- rarefy_even_depth(ps_glom_first,
                                      sample.size = 1500,
                                      rngseed = 1,
                                      replace = FALSE,
                                      verbose = FALSE)

# Distance matrix
dist_glom_rare <- distance(ps_glom_rarefied, method = "bray")
class(dist_glom_rare)
# Adonis2
adonis_glom_rare <- adonis2(dist_glom_rare ~ dysbiosis_binary,
                            data = as(sample_data(ps_glom_rarefied), "data.frame"))


adonis_glom_rare #3.856
adonis_rare_glom #3.999 




# Agglomerate first but now keep NAs 
ps_glom_first2 <- tax_glom(subset_1500, taxrank = "Family", NArm=FALSE)

# Rarefy after
ps_glom_rarefied2 <- rarefy_even_depth(ps_glom_first2,
                                      sample.size = 1500,
                                      rngseed = 1,
                                      replace = FALSE,
                                      verbose = FALSE)

# Distance matrix
dist_glom_rare2 <- phyloseq::distance(ps_glom_rarefied2, method = "bray")

# Adonis2
adonis_glom_rare2 <- adonis2(dist_glom_rare2 ~ dysbiosis_binary,
                            data = as(sample_data(ps_glom_rarefied2), "data.frame"))


adonis_glom_rare #3.856
adonis_rare_glom #3.999 
adonis_glom_rare2 #3.75

#Small differences but not drastic 

```

```{r}
#Sanity check does the % unknown differ between Dysbiotic and non-Dysbiotic samples 
ps_family_st <- tax_glom(subset_1500, taxrank = "Family", NArm = FALSE)
ps_family_rel_sc <- transform_sample_counts(ps_family_st, function(x) x / sum(x))

tax_st <- tax_table(ps_family_rel_sc)
unknown_taxa_st <- rownames(tax_st)[is.na(tax_st[, "Family"]) | tax_st[, "Family"] == ""]
unknown_taxa_st


otu_rel_st <- as.data.frame(otu_table(ps_family_rel_sc))
unknown_abund_st <- colSums(otu_rel_st[unknown_taxa_st, , drop = FALSE])
unknown_abund_st


meta_st <- as(sample_data(ps_family_rel_sc), "data.frame")
meta_st$unknown_rel_abund <- unknown_abund_st


aggregate(unknown_rel_abund ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta_st, mean)

#4.9% vs. 3.3% in Yes

```

```{r}
#"Final" version of PERMANOVA - FOR WHOLE VIROME PERMANOVA 
#MOST CORRECT - rarefying without replacement, without microviz, ensuring sample order is correct, and using restriction of Permutations within each individual

set.seed(1)
#VOTU LEVEL WHOLE VIROME 

rarefied.reads.1500.final <-  rarefy_even_depth(subset_1500, sample.size = min(sample_sums(subset_1500)),
  rngseed = 1, replace = FALSE, trimOTUs = TRUE, verbose = TRUE)

sample_sums(rarefied.reads.1500.final)

# Distance matrix
dist_rare_final <- distance(rarefied.reads.1500.final, method = "bray")
meta.final <- as(sample_data(rarefied.reads.1500.final), "data.frame")

class(dist_rare_final)
#convert character to factor
meta.final$Participant.ID <- as.factor(meta.final$Participant.ID)
meta.final$dysbiosis_binary <- as.factor(meta.final$dysbiosis_binary)

#adonis
set.seed(1)
adonis.dysbiosis.votu.final <- adonis2(dist_rare_final ~ meta.final$dysbiosis_binary,
                            data = meta.final, strata = meta.final$Participant.ID)



#the way that they do it here with permute package
library(permute)
set.seed(1)

h1_final_whole_virome <- with( meta.final, how(nperm = 999, blocks = Participant.ID))

adonis2(dist_rare_final ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta.final, permutations = h1_final_whole_virome)

#now restrict permutations to across individuals (permute plots) for diagnosis - but note: I cant do that because groups have to be equal for permutating plots 
h1_final_whole_virome_across <- with(meta.final, how(nperm = 999,
  plots = Plots(strata = Participant.ID, type = "free"),
  within = Within(type = "none")))

#adonis2(dist_rare_final ~ diagnosis.x, data = meta.final, permutations = h1_final_whole_virome_across)




#FAMILY LEVEL #######
#IM GOING TO TAX GLOM BEFORE I RAREFY BECAUSE IN THEORY THEY MAY NOT HAVE THE SAME NUMBER OF SEQUENCES AFTER REMOVING NA VOTUS (with no family classification)

final.tax.glom <- tax_glom(subset_1500, taxrank="Family", NArm = TRUE) 
min(sample_sums(final.tax.glom)) #rarefy at 1106 min number of reads 


rarefied.reads.1500.final.fam <-  rarefy_even_depth(final.tax.glom, sample.size = min(sample_sums(final.tax.glom)),
  rngseed = 1, replace = FALSE, trimOTUs = TRUE, verbose = TRUE)
sample_sums(rarefied.reads.1500.final.fam)


packageVersion("vegan")




# Distance matrix
dist_rare_final_fam <- distance(rarefied.reads.1500.final.fam, method = "bray")
meta.final.fam <- as(sample_data(rarefied.reads.1500.final.fam), "data.frame")

meta.final.fam$Participant.ID <- as.factor(meta.final.fam$Participant.ID)
meta.final.fam$dysbiosis_binary <- as.factor(meta.final.fam$dysbiosis_binary)

all(rownames(meta.final.fam) == colnames(as.matrix(dist_rare_final_fam)))

#adonis
adonis.dysbiosis.votu.final.fam <- adonis2(dist_rare_final_fam ~ meta.final.fam$dysbiosis_binary,
                            data = meta.final.fam, strata = meta.final.fam$Participant.ID)
any(is.na(meta.final.fam$dysbiosis_binary))
any(is.na(meta.final.fam$Participant.ID))
adonis.dysbiosis.votu.final.fam


library(permute)



block_fam_final <- with( meta.final.fam, how(nperm = 999, blocks = Participant.ID))
block_votu_final <-  with( meta.final, how(nperm = 999, blocks = Participant.ID))

set.seed(1)
adonis2(dist_rare_final_fam ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta.final.fam, permutations = block_fam_final) #3.9 R-squared - p=0.001

set.seed(1)
adonis2(dist_rare_final ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta.final, permutations = block_votu_final) #1.74 r-squared - p=0.001


#now do the same but remove any samples that dont have a dysbiotic AND non=dysbiotic - shouldnt impact p-value I believe because I think samples with only one observation arent included anyways in p-value checks 

yes_no_whole_virome_check_ps <- rarefied.reads.1500.final.fam
metadata_test_yes_no <-  as(sample_data(yes_no_whole_virome_check_ps), "data.frame")


yes_ids_1500_check <- metadata_test_yes_no %>% 
  filter(dysbiosis_binary == "Yes") %>% 
  distinct(Participant.ID)

no_ids_1500_check <- metadata_test_yes_no %>% 
  filter(dysbiosis_binary == "No") %>% 
  distinct(Participant.ID)


common_ids_whole_vir_yes_no <- intersect(yes_ids_1500_check$Participant.ID, no_ids_1500_check$Participant.ID)
length(common_ids_whole_vir_yes_no)

filtered_ps_whole_virome <- subset_samples(
  yes_no_whole_virome_check_ps, 
  Participant.ID %in% common_ids_whole_vir_yes_no
)






# Distance matrix
dist_rare_final_fam_yes_no <- distance(filtered_ps_whole_virome, method = "bray")
meta.final.fam_yes_no <- as(sample_data(filtered_ps_whole_virome), "data.frame")


meta.final.fam_yes_no$Participant.ID <- as.factor(meta.final.fam_yes_no$Participant.ID)
meta.final.fam_yes_no$dysbiosis_binary <- as.factor(meta.final.fam_yes_no$dysbiosis_binary)

all(rownames(meta.final.fam_yes_no) == colnames(as.matrix(dist_rare_final_fam_yes_no)))


block_fam_final_yes_no <- with( meta.final.fam_yes_no, how(nperm = 999, blocks = Participant.ID))


adonis2(dist_rare_final_fam_yes_no ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta.final.fam_yes_no, permutations = block_fam_final_yes_no) #2.8 R-squared - p=0.001

#now do the same but remove any samples that dont have a dysbiotic AND non=dysbiotic - shouldnt impact p-value I believe because I think samples with only one observation arent included anyways in p-value checks :vOTU 


yes_no_whole_virome_check_ps_votu <- rarefied.reads.1500.final
metadata_test_yes_no_votu <-  as(sample_data(yes_no_whole_virome_check_ps_votu), "data.frame")


yes_ids_1500_check_votu <- metadata_test_yes_no_votu %>% 
  filter(dysbiosis_binary == "Yes") %>% 
  distinct(Participant.ID)



no_ids_1500_check_votu <- metadata_test_yes_no_votu %>% 
  filter(dysbiosis_binary == "No") %>% 
  distinct(Participant.ID)


common_ids_whole_vir_yes_no_votu <- intersect(yes_ids_1500_check_votu$Participant.ID, no_ids_1500_check_votu$Participant.ID)
length(common_ids_whole_vir_yes_no_votu)

filtered_ps_whole_virome_votu <- subset_samples(
  yes_no_whole_virome_check_ps_votu, 
  Participant.ID %in% common_ids_whole_vir_yes_no_votu
)




# Distance matrix
dist_rare_final_fam_yes_no_votu <- distance(filtered_ps_whole_virome_votu, method = "bray")
meta.final.fam_yes_no_votu <- as(sample_data(filtered_ps_whole_virome_votu), "data.frame")


meta.final.fam_yes_no_votu$Participant.ID <- as.factor(meta.final.fam_yes_no_votu$Participant.ID)
meta.final.fam_yes_no_votu$dysbiosis_binary <- as.factor(meta.final.fam_yes_no_votu$dysbiosis_binary)

all(rownames(meta.final.fam_yes_no_votu) == colnames(as.matrix(dist_rare_final_fam_yes_no_votu)))



block_fam_final_yes_no_votu <- with( meta.final.fam_yes_no_votu, how(nperm = 999, blocks = Participant.ID))

set.seed(1)
adonis2(dist_rare_final_fam_yes_no_votu ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta.final.fam_yes_no_votu, permutations = block_fam_final_yes_no_votu) #2.8 R-squared - p=0.001



#No see what happens if I take out the non-IBD samples: 


rarefied.reads.1500.final.fam.CDUC.only <- subset_samples(rarefied.reads.1500.final.fam, diagnosis.x != "nonIBD")
#767 samples left 


packageVersion("vegan")


# Distance matrix
dist_rare_final_fam_UC_CD_only <- distance(rarefied.reads.1500.final.fam.CDUC.only, method = "bray")
meta.final.fam_UCCD <- as(sample_data(rarefied.reads.1500.final.fam.CDUC.only), "data.frame")

meta.final.fam_UCCD$Participant.ID <- as.factor(meta.final.fam_UCCD$Participant.ID)
meta.final.fam_UCCD$dysbiosis_binary <- as.factor(meta.final.fam_UCCD$dysbiosis_binary)

all(rownames(meta.final.fam_UCCD) == rownames(as.matrix(dist_rare_final_fam_UC_CD_only)))

length(rownames(meta.final.fam_UCCD))
length(rownames(as.matrix(dist_rare_final_fam_UC_CD_only)))
sum(is.na(meta.final.fam_UCCD$dysbiosis_binary))
sum(is.na(meta.final.fam_UCCD$Patricipant.ID))


meta.final.fam_UCCD
Participant.ID
meta_30 <- rownames(meta.final.fam_UCCD)[30]

# 30th sample ID in distance matrix
dist_30 <- rownames(as.matrix(dist_rare_final_fam_UC_CD_only))[30]


table(meta.final.fam_UCCD$diagnosis.x) #confirmed no non-IBD

block_fam_final_fam_UCCD <- with(meta.final.fam_UCCD, how(nperm = 999, blocks = Participant.ID))


class(dist_rare_final_fam_UC_CD_only)
set.seed(1)
adonis2(dist_rare_final_fam_UC_CD_only ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta.final.fam_UCCD, permutations = block_fam_final_fam_UCCD) #4.774 (higher than before filtering for just CD & UC) R-squared - p=0.001

head(dist_rare_final_fam_UC_CD_only)
library(dada2)

adonis_pq(rarefied.reads.1500.final.fam.CDUC.only, "dysbiosis_binary", dist_method = "bray")

```{r}
#TEMPERATE FAMILY PERMANOVA AND PCOA 
#SUBSETTING AT 1500 READS OR ABOVE 
#TAX GLOMMING 
#DETERMINING NUMBERS OF SEQS/SAMPLE AFTER GLOMMING 
#THEN REMOVE SAMPLES WITH LOW READS 
#THEN RAREFY AT MIN DEPTH, BRAY-CURTIS DISTANCE AND PERMANOVA WITH  BLOCKING 



min(sample_sums(physeqhmp2tempvir))


#TEMP SUBSET 1500 
#HERE WE ARE USING THE LENGTH_NORMALIZED READ COUNT WHICH COMES FROM THE INITIAL METADATA - SO WE ARE USING THE CUTOFF FOR THE WHOLE VIROME - THIS IS WHY THE NUMBER OF SAMPLES IS THE SAME AS subset_1500
subset_1500.temp <-  subset_samples(physeqhmp2tempvir, length_normalized_read_count > 1500)
subset_1500.temp

#because we are losing seqs after lets look at the number of sequences 
num_seqs_temp <- as.data.frame(sample_sums(subset_1500.temp))

#order in ascending 
num_seqs_temp <- num_seqs_temp[order(num_seqs_temp$`sample_sums(subset_1500.temp)`), ]

#more relevant will be the seqs after glomming because we will lose some here 

subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first <- tax_glom(subset_1500.temp, taxrank="Family", NArm = TRUE) 

sort(sample_sums(subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first))
#because we are losing seqs after lets look at the number of sequences 
num_seqs_temp.fam <- as.data.frame(sample_sums(subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first))

#order in ascending 
num_seqs_temp.fam <- num_seqs_temp.fam[order(num_seqs_temp.fam$`sample_sums(subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first)`), ]

#MOST SAMPLES ABOVE 1000, BUT FOR THE SAKE OF KEEPING MORE VIRULENT SAMPLES WHICH HAVE LESS READ DEPTH I WILL REMOVE SAMPLES WITH LESS THAN 700 # ONE BELOW 100, a few below 700
#ALso because we are rarefying it makes sense to not keep samples with ie- 57 reads





#remove low read samples - USING 700 Arbitrarily (remember this is after glomming)


ps.filtered.temp.fam <- prune_samples(sample_sums(subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first) >= 700, subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first)
ps.filtered.temp.fam #1093 to 1079 samples 



#check that it worked 
num_seqs_temp.fam.filter <- as.data.frame(sample_sums(ps.filtered.temp.fam))

set.seed(1)
#now rarefy and do Bray PERMANOVA
#RAREFY WITHOUT REPLACEMENT
rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first <-   rarefy_even_depth(ps.filtered.temp.fam, sample.size = min(sample_sums(ps.filtered.temp.fam)),
  rngseed = 1, replace = FALSE, trimOTUs = TRUE, verbose = TRUE)








# Distance matrix
dist_rare_final_fam_temp <- phyloseq::distance(rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first, method = "bray")
meta.final.fam.fam_temp <- as(sample_data(rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first), "data.frame")
all(rownames(as.matrix(dist_rare_final_fam_temp)) == rownames(meta.final.fam.fam_temp))

meta.final.fam.fam_temp$Participant.ID <- as.factor(meta.final.fam.fam_temp$Participant.ID)
meta.final.fam.fam_temp$dysbiosis_binary <- as.factor(meta.final.fam.fam_temp$dysbiosis_binary)

all(rownames(as.matrix(dist_rare_final_fam_temp)) == rownames(meta.final.fam.fam_temp))

#adonis
adonis.dysbiosis.fam.final.temp <- adonis2(dist_rare_final_fam_temp ~ dysbiosis_binary,
                            data = meta.final.fam.fam_temp, strata = meta.final.fam.fam_temp$Participant.ID)



#then doing it this way: 

set.seed(1)
block_fam_temp <- with(meta.final.fam.fam_temp, how(nperm = 999, blocks = Participant.ID))
adonis2(dist_rare_final_fam_temp ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta.final.fam.fam_temp, permutations = block_fam_temp)
adonis2(dist_rare_final_fam_temp ~ temp_length_normalized_read_count, data = meta.final.fam.fam_temp, permutations = block_fam_temp)


#See if things change with higher permutations 
#block_fam_temp2 <- with(meta.final.fam.fam_temp, how(nperm = 10000, blocks = Participant.ID))
#adonis2(dist_rare_final_fam_temp ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta.final.fam.fam_temp, permutations = block_fam_temp2)



#bd_temp_simple <- betadisper(dist_rare_final_fam_temp, meta.final.fam.fam_temp$dysbiosis_binary)
#anova(bd_temp_simple)         # ANOVA test
#permutest(bd_temp_simple)     # Permutation test
#permutest(bd_temp_simple, permutations = block_fam_temp) #this might be relevant for blocking? 


#adonis.dysbiosis.fam.final.temp
#adonis.dysbiosis.fam.final.temp.length <- adonis2(dist_rare_final_fam_temp ~ meta.final.fam.fam_temp$temp_length_normalized_read_count,
                      #      data = meta.final.fam.fam_temp, strata = meta.final.fam.fam_temp$Participant.ID)




#

#PCOA 

sam_names <- sample_names(rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first)

# 2. Run PCoA
pcoa_ord_temp_final <- ordinate(rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first, method = "PCoA", distance = dist_rare_final_fam_temp)

# 3. Plot
p_temp_family_virome_final <- plot_ordination(rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first, pcoa_ord_temp_final, color = "dysbiosis_binary") +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "PCoA (Bray–Curtis)")


#And now do the same for yes and no only


#now do the same but remove any samples that dont have a dysbiotic AND non=dysbiotic - shouldnt impact p-value I believe because I think samples with only one observation arent included anyways in p-value checks 

yes_no_temp_virome_check_ps <- rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first
metadata_test_yes_no_temp <-  as(sample_data(yes_no_temp_virome_check_ps), "data.frame")


yes_ids_1500_check_temp <- metadata_test_yes_no_temp %>% 
  filter(dysbiosis_binary == "Yes") %>% 
  distinct(Participant.ID)

no_ids_1500_check_temp <- metadata_test_yes_no_temp %>% 
  filter(dysbiosis_binary == "No") %>% 
  distinct(Participant.ID)


common_ids_whole_vir_yes_no_temp <- intersect(yes_ids_1500_check_temp$Participant.ID, no_ids_1500_check_temp$Participant.ID)
length(common_ids_whole_vir_yes_no_temp)

filtered_ps_temp_virome <- subset_samples(
  yes_no_temp_virome_check_ps, 
  Participant.ID %in% common_ids_whole_vir_yes_no_temp
)




# Distance matrix
dist_rare_final_fam_temp_yes_no <- phyloseq::distance(filtered_ps_temp_virome, method = "bray")
meta.final.fam.fam_temp_yes_no <- as(sample_data(filtered_ps_temp_virome), "data.frame")
all(rownames(as.matrix(dist_rare_final_fam_temp_yes_no)) == rownames(meta.final.fam.fam_temp_yes_no))

meta.final.fam.fam_temp_yes_no$Participant.ID <- as.factor(meta.final.fam.fam_temp_yes_no$Participant.ID)
meta.final.fam.fam_temp_yes_no$dysbiosis_binary <- as.factor(meta.final.fam.fam_temp_yes_no$dysbiosis_binary)

all(rownames(as.matrix(dist_rare_final_fam_temp_yes_no)) == rownames(meta.final.fam.fam_temp_yes_no))


#then doing it this way: 
set.seed(1)
block_fam_temp_yes_no <- with(meta.final.fam.fam_temp_yes_no, how(nperm = 999, blocks = Participant.ID))
adonis2(dist_rare_final_fam_temp_yes_no ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta.final.fam.fam_temp_yes_no, permutations = block_fam_temp_yes_no) #2.75






#NOW READ IN TEMPERATE READ COUNT PRE-RAREFYING AS A CATEGORY

rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first2 <- rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first
###ADD IN  READ DEPTH BEFORE RAREFYING AS CATEGORY OF METADATA
meta_rare_temp <- as(sample_data(rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first2), "data.frame")
meta_unrar_temp <- as(sample_data(ps.filtered.temp.fam), "data.frame")


meta_unrar_temp$orig_depth <- sample_sums(ps.filtered.temp.fam)
meta_rare_temp$SampleID  <- rownames(meta_rare_temp)
meta_unrar_temp$SampleID <- rownames(meta_unrar_temp)


meta_joined_temp <- merge(
  meta_rare_temp,
  meta_unrar_temp[, c("SampleID", "orig_depth")],
  by = "SampleID",
  all.x = TRUE
)


#now with updated metadata


rownames(meta_joined_temp) <- meta_joined_temp$SampleID
meta_joined_temp <- meta_joined_temp[sample_names(rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first2), ]

sample_data(rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first2) <- meta_joined_temp

#sanity check
sample_data(rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first2) #H4028_8_CD_P  978
sample_sums(ps.filtered.temp.fam)["H4028_8_CD_P"]

sample_data(rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first2) #P6012_23_1_UC  1132

sample_sums(ps.filtered.temp.fam)["P6012_23_1_UC"] #1132
```

  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "PCoA (Bray–Curtis)")


plot_ordination(rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.firstCDUC2, ordination_temp_CDCUC2, color = "orig_depth") +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "PCoA (Bray–Curtis)")



plot_ordination(
  rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.firstCDUC_plot, 
  ordination_temp_CDCUC, 
  color = "dysbiosis_binary"
) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors_dysb) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "PCoA (Bray–Curtis)")

```

```{r}
#Sanity check does the % unknown differ between Dysbiotic and non-Dysbiotic samples - TEMP
ps_family_st_temp <- tax_glom(subset_1500.temp, taxrank = "Family", NArm = FALSE)
ps_family_rel_sc_temp <- transform_sample_counts(ps_family_st_temp, function(x) x / sum(x))

tax_st_temp <- tax_table(ps_family_rel_sc_temp)
unknown_taxa_st_temp <- rownames(tax_st_temp)[is.na(tax_st_temp[, "Family"]) | tax_st_temp[, "Family"] == ""]
unknown_taxa_st_temp


otu_rel_st_temp <- as.data.frame(otu_table(ps_family_rel_sc_temp))
unknown_abund_st_temp <- colSums(otu_rel_st_temp[unknown_taxa_st_temp, , drop = FALSE])
unknown_abund_st_temp


meta_st_temp <- as(sample_data(ps_family_rel_sc_temp), "data.frame")
meta_st_temp$unknown_rel_abund <- unknown_abund_st_temp


aggregate(unknown_rel_abund ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta_st_temp, mean)


#SANITY CHECK WHAT IF I DONT REMOVE UNKNOWNS


subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first.keepNA.test <- tax_glom(subset_1500.temp, taxrank="Family", NArm = TRUE) 

subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first.keepNA <- tax_glom(subset_1500.temp, taxrank="Family", NArm = FALSE) 

sort(sample_sums(subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first.keepNA))
sort(sample_sums(subset_1500.temp))



#MOST SAMPLES ABOVE 1000, BUT FOR THE SAKE OF KEEPING MORE VIRULENT SAMPLES WHICH HAVE LESS READ DEPTH I WILL REMOVE SAMPLES WITH LESS THAN 700 # ONE BELOW 100, a few below 700
#ALso because we are rarefying it makes sense to not keep samples with ie- 57 reads



ps.filtered.temp.fam.NAKEEP <- prune_samples(sample_sums(subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first.keepNA) >= 700, subset_1500.temp.fam.glom.first.keepNA)
ps.filtered.temp.fam #1093 to 1081 samples 



#check that it worked
set.seed(1)
#now rarefy and do Bray PERMANOVA
#RAREFY WITHOUT REPLACEMENT
rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first.NAKEEP <-   rarefy_even_depth(ps.filtered.temp.fam.NAKEEP, sample.size = min(sample_sums(ps.filtered.temp.fam.NAKEEP)),
  rngseed = 1, replace = FALSE, trimOTUs = TRUE, verbose = TRUE)





rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first.NAKEEP <- subset_samples(rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first.NAKEEP, diagnosis.x != "nonIBD")


distanceMethodList

# Distance matrix

dist_rare_final_fam_temp.NAKEEP <- phyloseq::distance(rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first.NAKEEP, method = "bray")
meta.final.fam.fam_temp.NAKEEP <- as(sample_data(rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first.NAKEEP), "data.frame")
all(rownames(as.matrix(dist_rare_final_fam_temp.NAKEEP)) == rownames(meta.final.fam.fam_temp.NAKEEP))

meta.final.fam.fam_temp.NAKEEP$Participant.ID <- as.factor(meta.final.fam.fam_temp.NAKEEP$Participant.ID)
meta.final.fam.fam_temp.NAKEEP$dysbiosis_binary <- as.factor(meta.final.fam.fam_temp.NAKEEP$dysbiosis_binary)

all(rownames(as.matrix(dist_rare_final_fam_temp.NAKEEP)) == rownames(meta.final.fam.fam_temp.NAKEEP))

#adonis
 adonis2(dist_rare_final_fam_temp.NAKEEP ~ dysbiosis_binary,
                            data = meta.final.fam.fam_temp.NAKEEP, strata = meta.final.fam.fam_temp.NAKEEP$Participant.ID)

#4.3 instead of 4.4
 
 s23
