```{r}
#Now do the same with virulent 

subset_1500.vir <-  subset_samples(physeqhmp2vir, length_normalized_read_count > 1500)


subset_1500.virulent.fam.glom.first <- tax_glom(subset_1500.vir, taxrank="Family", NArm = TRUE) 


#because we are losing seqs after lets look at the number of sequences 
num_seqs_vir.fam <- as.data.frame(sample_sums(subset_1500.virulent.fam.glom.first))

#order in ascending 
num_seqs_vir.fam <- num_seqs_vir.fam[order(num_seqs_vir.fam$`sample_sums(subset_1500.virulent.fam.glom.first)`), ]
#ALOT MORE LESS THAN 700 in this case (probably removing about 500 samples )


ps.filtered.vir.fam <- prune_samples(sample_sums(subset_1500.virulent.fam.glom.first) >= 700, subset_1500.virulent.fam.glom.first)
sample_sums(ps.filtered.vir.fam)
#down to 589 samples 


#RAREFY WITHOUT REPLACEMENT
rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.first <-   rarefy_even_depth(ps.filtered.vir.fam, sample.size = min(sample_sums(ps.filtered.vir.fam)),
  rngseed = 1, replace = FALSE, trimOTUs = TRUE, verbose = TRUE)
sample_sums(rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.first)



# Distance matrix
dist_rare_final_fam_virulent <- phyloseq::distance(rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.first, method = "bray")
meta.final.fam.fam_virulent <- as(sample_data(rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.first), "data.frame")


all(rownames(as.matrix(dist_rare_final_fam_virulent)) == rownames(meta.final.fam.fam_virulent))

meta.final.fam.fam_virulent$Participant.ID <- as.factor(meta.final.fam.fam_virulent$Participant.ID)
meta.final.fam.fam_virulent$dysbiosis_binary <- as.factor(meta.final.fam.fam_virulent$dysbiosis_binary)

all(rownames(as.matrix(dist_rare_final_fam_virulent)) == rownames(meta.final.fam.fam_virulent))
#adonis
adonis.dysbiosis.fam.final.vir <- adonis2(dist_rare_final_fam_virulent ~ meta.final.fam.fam_virulent$dysbiosis_binary,
                            data = meta.final.fam.fam_virulent, strata = meta.final.fam.fam_virulent$Participant.ID)



block_vir <- with(meta.final.fam.fam_virulent, how(nperm = 999, blocks = Participant.ID))
adonis2(dist_rare_final_fam_virulent ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta.final.fam.fam_virulent, permutations = block_vir)


#Run beta dipsersion - to my knowledge it cant handle repeated measures though 
bd_vir_simple <- betadisper(dist_rare_final_fam_virulent, meta.final.fam.fam_virulent$dysbiosis_binary)
anova(bd_vir_simple)         # ANOVA test
permutest(bd_vir_simple)     # Permutation test
permutest(bd_vir_simple, permutations = block_vir) #this might be relevant for blocking? 




###ADD IN VIRULENT READ DEPTH BEFORE RAREFYING AS CATEGORY OF METADATA
rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.first2 <- rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.first

meta_rare_vir <- as(sample_data(rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.first2), "data.frame")
meta_unrar_vir <- as(sample_data(ps.filtered.vir.fam), "data.frame")

ps.filtered.vir.fam
rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.first

meta_unrar_vir$orig_depth <- sample_sums(ps.filtered.vir.fam)
meta_rare_vir$SampleID  <- rownames(meta_rare_vir)
meta_unrar_vir$SampleID <- rownames(meta_unrar_vir)



meta_joined_vir <- merge(
  meta_rare_vir,
  meta_unrar_vir[, c("SampleID", "orig_depth")],
  by = "SampleID",
  all.x = TRUE
)

#now with updated metadata


rownames(meta_joined_vir) <- meta_joined_vir$SampleID
meta_joined_vir <- meta_joined_vir[sample_names(rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.first2), ]

sample_data(rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.first2) <- meta_joined_vir






# Distance matrix
dist_rare_final_fam_virulent <- phyloseq::distance(rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.first2, method = "bray")

all(rownames(as.matrix(dist_rare_final_fam_virulent)) == rownames(meta.final.fam.fam_virulent))

meta.final.fam.fam_virulent$Participant.ID <- as.factor(meta.final.fam.fam_virulent$Participant.ID)
meta.final.fam.fam_virulent$dysbiosis_binary <- as.factor(meta.final.fam.fam_virulent$dysbiosis_binary)
all(rownames(as.matrix(dist_rare_final_fam_virulent)) == rownames(meta.final.fam.fam_virulent))
#adonis




block_vir <- with(meta.final.fam.fam_virulent, how(nperm = 999, blocks = Participant.ID))
adonis2(dist_rare_final_fam_virulent ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta.final.fam.fam_virulent, permutations = block_vir)


adonis2(dist_rare_final_fam_virulent ~ orig_depth,
                            data = meta.final.fam.fam_virulent, permutations = block_vir)


adonis2(
  dist_rare_final_fam_virulent ~ dysbiosis_binary + orig_depth,

boxplot(orig_depth ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta.final.fam.fam_virulent)
wilcox.test(orig_depth ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta.final.fam.fam_virulent)

yes_ids_1500_check_vir <- metadata_test_yes_no_vir %>% 
  filter(dysbiosis_binary == "Yes") %>% 
  distinct(Participant.ID)

no_ids_1500_check_vir <- metadata_test_yes_no_vir %>% 
  filter(dysbiosis_binary == "No") %>% 
  distinct(Participant.ID)


common_ids_whole_vir_yes_no_vir <- intersect(yes_ids_1500_check_vir$Participant.ID, no_ids_1500_check_vir$Participant.ID)

)




# Distance matrix

#then doing it this way:

# Distance matrix

meta.final.fam.fam_virulentCDUC <- as(sample_data(rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.firstCDUC), "data.frame")


all(rownames(as.matrix(dist_rare_final_fam_virulentCDUC)) == rownames(meta.final.fam.fam_virulentCDUC))

meta.final.fam.fam_virulentCDUC$dysbiosis_binary <- as.factor(meta.final.fam.fam_virulentCDUC$dysbiosis_binary)

all(rownames(as.matrix(dist_rare_final_fam_virulentCDUC)) == rownames(meta.final.fam.fam_virulentCDUC))
rownames(as.matrix(dist_rare_final_fam_virulentCDUC))[300]

block_fam_virCDUC <- with(meta.final.fam.fam_virulentCDUC, how(nperm = 999, blocks = Participant.ID))
adonis2(dist_rare_final_fam_virulentCDUC ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta.final.fam.fam_virulentCDUC, permutations = block_fam_virCDUC) #3.36





table(meta.final.fam.fam_virulentCDUC$dysbiosis_binary)
adonis2(dist_rare_final_fam_virulentCDUC ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta.final.fam.fam_virulentCDUC) #3.36
adonis_pq(rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.firstCDUC, "dysbiosis_binary", dist_method = "jaccard")
adonis_pq(rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.firstCDUC, "dysbiosis_binary", dist_method = "bray")



#PCoA 
rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.firstCDUC_plot <- rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.firstCDUC

dist_rare_final_fam_virCDUC_plot <- distance(rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.firstCDUC_plot, method = "bray")
ordination_virulent_CDCUC <- ordinate(rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.firstCDUC_plot, method = "PCoA", distance = dist_rare_final_fam_virCDUC_plot)

sample_data(rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.firstCDUC_plot)$dysbiosis_binary <- 
  factor(sample_data(rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.firstCDUC_plot)$dysbiosis_binary,
         levels = c("No", "Yes"),
         labels = c("Non-Dysbiotic", "Dysbiotic"))


custom_colors_dysb <- c("Non-Dysbiotic" = "#B59410", "Dysbiotic" = "forestgreen")

# Extract % variance explained on first two axes
# Sometimes it's under $values$Eigenvalues but Relative_eig gives % directly in vegan-based ordinations


# Create custom axis labels
vir_dysb_plot <- plot_ordination(
  rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.firstCDUC_plot, 
  ordination_virulent_CDCUC, 
  color = "dysbiosis_binary"
) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors_dysb) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(      # Remove title, change legend title
    title = NULL,
    color = "Dysbiosis Status"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 17),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 20)  # for legend title if you want it bigger
  ) + theme(
  panel.grid = element_blank()
)








###ADD IN VIRULENT  READ DEPTH BEFORE RAREFYING AS CATEGORY OF METADATA
rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.firstCDUC2 <- rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.firstCDUC
meta_rare_vir2 <- as(sample_data(rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.firstCDUC2), "data.frame")
meta_unrar_vir2 <- as(sample_data(ps.filtered.vir.fam), "data.frame")


meta_unrar_vir2$orig_depth <- sample_sums(ps.filtered.vir.fam)
meta_rare_vir2$SampleID  <- rownames(meta_rare_vir2)
meta_unrar_vir2$SampleID <- rownames(meta_unrar_vir2)


meta_joined_vir2 <- merge(
  meta_rare_vir2,
  meta_unrar_vir2[, c("SampleID", "orig_depth")],
  by = "SampleID",
  all.x = TRUE
)

table(meta_joined_vir2$dysbiosis_binary)
#now with updated metadata

rownames(meta_joined_vir2) <- meta_joined_vir2$SampleID
meta_joined_vir2 <- meta_joined_vir2[sample_names(rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.firstCDUC2), ]

sample_data(rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.firstCDUC2) <- meta_joined_vir2

rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.firstCDUC2


#check that it matches up: 




# Distance matrix
dist_rare_final_fam_virCDUC2 <- distance(rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.firstCDUC2, method = "bray")

meta.final.fam.fam_virCDUC2 <- as(sample_data(rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.firstCDUC2), "data.frame")

all(rownames(as.matrix(meta.final.fam.fam_virCDUC2)) == rownames(dist_rare_final_fam_virCDUC2))

all(rownames(meta.final.fam.fam_virCDUC2) == labels(dist_rare_final_fam_virCDUC2))


meta.final.fam.fam_virCDUC2$Participant.ID <- as.factor(meta.final.fam.fam_virCDUC2$Participant.ID)
meta.final.fam.fam_virCDUC2$dysbiosis_binary <- as.factor(meta.final.fam.fam_virCDUC2$dysbiosis_binary)

all(rownames(as.matrix(dist_rare_final_fam_virCDUC2)) == rownames(meta.final.fam.fam_virCDUC2))

#adonis

#then doing it this way: 

block_fam_virCDUC2 <- with(meta.final.fam.fam_virCDUC2, how(nperm = 999, blocks = Participant.ID))
adonis2(dist_rare_final_fam_virCDUC2 ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta.final.fam.fam_virCDUC2, permutations = block_fam_virCDUC2) #3.36


#and adding depth as a marginal effect 
adonis2(
  dist_rare_final_fam_virCDUC2 ~ dysbiosis_binary + orig_depth,
  data = meta.final.fam.fam_virCDUC2,
  permutations = block_fam_virCDUC2 ,
  by = "margin"
)

table(meta.final.fam.fam_virCDUC2$dysbiosis_binary)

table(meta.final.fam.fam_virCDUC2$diagnosis.x)
```

```{r}
#adonis
 adonis2(dist_rare_final_fam_temp.NAKEEP.vir ~ dysbiosis_binary,
                            data = meta.final.fam.fam_temp.NAKEEP.vir, strata = meta.final.fam.fam_temp.NAKEEP.vir$Participant.ID)
#3.1 instead of 3.3 
 block_fam_virCDUC2.2 <- with(meta.final.fam.fam_temp.NAKEEP.vir, how(nperm = 999, blocks = Participant.ID))

adonis2(dist_rare_final_fam_temp.NAKEEP.vir ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta.final.fam.fam_temp.NAKEEP.vir, permutations = block_fam_virCDUC2.2) 

```

```{r}
#SANITY CHECK: RAREFIED TEMPERATE 

#how do Goods coverage and rarefaction look at the rarefied 700 
rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first


#AND ADD GOODS FOR RAREFIED PS 

ps_melt_fam.temp.final.rar <-  ps_melt(rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first) 




 goods.data.fam.temp.final.rar <- ps_melt_fam.temp.final.rar %>% 
  group_by(Sample) %>%
  summarise(nseqs= sum(Abundance),
            n_singletons = sum(Abundance == 1), 
            goods = 100*(1- n_singletons/nseqs)) 

ps_melt_fam_temp.final.rar.plot <- goods.data.fam.temp.final.rar %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = nseqs, y = goods)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100))

goods.data.fam.temp.final.rar <- na.omit(goods.data.fam.temp.final.rar)

mean(goods.data.fam.temp.final.rar$goods) #99.73
median(goods.data.fam.temp.final.rar$goods) #99.72


#AND ADD GOODS FOR RAREFIED PS - with JUST CD AND UC  


rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.firstCDUC2_melt <- ps_melt(rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.firstCDUC2)



 rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.firstCDUC2_melt_goods <- rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.firstCDUC2_melt %>% 
  group_by(Sample) %>%
  summarise(nseqs= sum(Abundance),
            n_singletons = sum(Abundance == 1), 
            goods = 100*(1- n_singletons/nseqs)) 
rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.firstCDUC2_melt_goods <- na.omit(rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.firstCDUC2_melt_goods)

mean(rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.firstCDUC2_melt_goods$goods) #99.75
median(rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.firstCDUC2_melt_goods$goods) #99.72

#RAREFACTION CURVE 

mat.fam.temp.final <- as(t(otu_table(rarefied.reads.filtered.temp.fam.glom.first)), "matrix")



random_rows.fam.temp.final <- sample(nrow(mat.fam.temp.final), 50)
matrix.sub.fam.temp.final <- mat.fam.temp.final[random_rows.fam.temp.final, ]


subset_mat.pre.filt.fam.temp.final <- matrix.sub.fam.temp.final[1:50, ]

rarecurve(subset_mat.pre.filt.fam.temp.final, step = 20, col = "coral", cex = 0.6, label = FALSE, main="Family Level")


#V2

# --- 1. Subset to 50 random samples ---
set.seed(1)   # for reproducibility
samples_50 <- sample_names(rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.first) %>% sample(50)
ps_50 <- prune_samples(samples_50, rarefied.reads.filtered.virulent.fam.glom.first)

# --- 2. Create rarefaction curve (vegan::rarecurve wrapped in phyloseq) ---
otu_mat <- as(otu_table(ps_50), "matrix")
if (taxa_are_rows(ps_50)) {
  otu_mat <- t(otu_mat)
}

# vegan's rarecurve function
rarecurve_list <- vegan::rarecurve(
  otu_mat,
  step = 20,
  sample = min(rowSums(otu_mat)),
  label = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
#family
rarefied.reads.1500.temp.1 <-  rarefy_even_depth(subset_1500.temp, sample.size = min(sample_sums(subset_1500.temp)),
  rngseed = 1, replace = TRUE, trimOTUs = TRUE, verbose = TRUE)








family.temp.sanity.rar <- tax_glom(rarefied.reads.1500.temp.1, taxrank="Family", NArm = TRUE) 











# 1. Extract metadata from your phyloseq object
meta.fam.sanity.temp <- as.data.frame(sample_data(family.temp.sanity.rar))

# 2. Create a categorical version of 'length_normalized_read_count'
meta.fam.sanity.temp$DepthCategory <- ifelse(meta.fam.sanity.temp$length_normalized_read_count < 2500, "low", "high")

# (Optional: convert to factor so ordering is consistent)
meta.fam.sanity.temp$DepthCategory <- factor(meta.fam.sanity.temp$DepthCategory, levels = c("low", "high"))

# 3. Replace the sample_data in your phyloseq object
sample_data(family.temp.sanity.rar)$DepthCategory <- meta.fam.sanity.temp$DepthCategory


identical(rownames(meta.fam.sanity.temp), sample_names(family.temp.sanity.rar))

# 4. Reuse your previous ordination
bray.sanity.temp.fam <- phyloseq::distance(family.temp.sanity.rar, method = "bray")
as.matrix(bray.sanity.temp.fam)
ord.sanity.temp.fam <- ordinate(family.temp.sanity.rar, method = "PCoA", distance = bray.sanity.temp.fam)

# 5. Plot PCoA colored by the new categorical variable
plot_ordination(family.temp.sanity.rar, ord.sanity.temp.fam, color = "DepthCategory") +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "C") +
  labs(color = "Length-normalized\nread count category")

plot_ordination(family.temp.sanity.rar, ord.sanity.temp.fam, color = "dysbiosis_binary") +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "C") +
  labs(color = "dysbiosis_binary")


meta.fam.sanity.temp <- as(sample_data(family.temp.sanity.rar), "data.frame")
adonis2(bray.sanity.temp.fam ~ length_normalized_read_count, data = meta.fam.sanity.temp, permutations = 999)
adonis2(bray.sanity.temp.fam ~ dysbiosis_binary, data = meta.fam.sanity.temp, permutations = 999)








#Now for family level - see if Good's coverage makes an impact 

#1 make my sample column rownames 
goods.data.fam.2 <- as.data.frame(goods.data.fam)
rownames(goods.data.fam.2) <- goods.data.fam.2$Sample
goods.data.fam.2$Sample <- NULL  # optional: remove first column


#2 check sample names align 

all(rownames(goods.data.fam.2) %in% sample_names(family.sanity))
identical(rownames(goods.data.fam.2), sample_names(family.sanity))



# Reorder the dataframe to match phyloseq sample order
goods.data.fam.2 <- goods.data.fam.2[sample_names(family.sanity), , drop = FALSE]
identical(rownames(goods.data.fam.2), sample_names(family.sanity))

# Then add the column
sample_data(family.sanity)$goods <- goods.data.fam.2$goods
meta.fam.sanity.2 <- as(sample_data(family.sanity), "data.frame")

adonis2(bray.sanity.fam.3 ~ goods, data = meta.fam.sanity.2, permutations = 999)




# 4. Reuse your previous ordination
bray.sanity.fam.4 <- phyloseq::distance(family.sanity, method = "bray")
as.matrix(bray.sanity.fam.4)
ord3.sanity.fam.4 <- ordinate(family.sanity, method = "PCoA", distance = bray.sanity.fam.4)

# 5. Plot PCoA colored by the new categorical variable
plot_ordination(family.sanity, ord3.sanity.fam.4, color = "goods") +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "C") +
  labs(color = "Goods")
```

```{r}

library(vegan)
library(multivariance)
library(viridis)


#metadata
metadata_df <- read.csv("/Users/anshulsinha/Desktop/IBD_prophages_aim/bioinformatics_aim/bioinformatics_HMP_2/prop_stats_outputs/combined_metadata.csv", header = TRUE)
metadata_df
names(metadata_df)[names(metadata_df) == 'Sample_name'] <- 'filename'


#remove samples that have no dysbiosis scores- this brings us to 1595 samples 
metadata_df <- metadata_df[complete.cases(metadata_df$dysbiosis_binary), ]



#create a function that generates distance between samples 

dist_calculator <- function(ps, dist, metadata) {
  
  
  
fixed_taxa <- ps %>%
 tax_fix(unknowns = "NA")

  
 dists <- fixed_taxa %>%
  dist_calc(dist) %>%
   dist_get() 
   
     
dists <- as.matrix(dists)
 
 melted_vOTU <- dists  %>%
   melt()
 
 melted_vOTU
  
 

 colnames(melted_vOTU) <- c("sample1", "sample2", "dist.vOTU")

 
  
 fam <- tax_glom(ps, taxrank="Family", NArm = TRUE) 
fixed_taxa_fam <- fam %>%
 tax_fix(unknowns = "NA")
 fam
 
 
 
 dist_fam <- fam %>%
  dist_calc(dist) %>%
   dist_get() 
 
 
  dist_fam <- as.matrix(dist_fam)

  melted_PBF <- dist_fam  %>%
   melt()
  
  
  
   colnames(melted_PBF) <- c("sample1", "sample2", "dist.pbf")

   
   
   dist.merged.all <- left_join(melted_vOTU %>% select(sample1, sample2, dist.vOTU), 
                         melted_PBF %>% select(sample1, sample2, dist.pbf), 
                         by = c("sample1" = "sample1", "sample2" = "sample2")) %>% 
                       left_join(metadata %>% select(Participant.ID, filename), by = c("sample1" = "filename")) %>% 
                       left_join(metadata %>% select(Participant.ID, filename), by = c("sample2" = "filename")) %>% 
                        melt()


colnames(dist.merged.all) <- c("sample1", "sample2", "subject1", "subject2", "metric", "value")
dist.merged.all

dist.merged.all$metric <- factor(dist.merged.all$metric, levels = c("dist.vOTU", "dist.pbf"))

#Same subject column to allow to dinstinguish between same v same vs. same vs different comparisons. 

dist.merged.all <- dist.merged.all %>% mutate(same.subject = subject1 == subject2)


dist.merged.all
# Remove rows where 'Value' is equal to 0 because this is same v same comparison  
dist.merged.all <- dist.merged.all[dist.merged.all$value != 0, ]
dist.merged.all



#change names 


dist.merged.all$same.subject = as.character(dist.merged.all$same.subject)
dist.merged.all$metric = as.character(dist.merged.all$metric)




dist.merged.all$same.subject[dist.merged.all$same.subject == "TRUE"] = "Intra-Individual"
dist.merged.all$same.subject[dist.merged.all$same.subject == "FALSE"] = "Inter-Individual"


dist.merged.all$metric[dist.merged.all$metric == "dist.vOTU"] = "vOTU"
dist.merged.all$metric[dist.merged.all$metric == "dist.pbf"] = "PBF"

colors <- c("#C3B1E1", "#a0d6b4")



dist.merged.all$metric <- factor(dist.merged.all$metric, levels = c("vOTU", "PBF"))



dist.merged.all
#confirm that we have duplicate differences 

dist.merged.all.delete <- dist.merged.all
#creates a column that merges the sample 1 and 2 cols in alph order, collapses the. sample1vs2 and sample2vs1 should have the same thing 
dist.merged.all.delete$sorted_pair <- apply(dist.merged.all.delete[, c("sample1", "sample2")], 1, function(x) paste(sort(x), collapse = "_"))
#test shows that for this pair there are two vOTU and two PBF rows with identical values 
dist.merged.all.delete.test <- dist.merged.all.delete[dist.merged.all.delete$sorted_pair == "C3001_0_CD_P_C3001_12_CD_P",]


# Group by 'sorted_pair' and 'metric' to keep one row for each combination




dist.merged.all.delete <- dist.merged.all.delete %>%
  group_by(sorted_pair, metric) %>%
  dplyr::slice(1) %>%  # Keeps the first row for each group
  ungroup()
   
 
dist.merged.all.delete
}



#create the distance dataframes for aitchison and bray distance 

aitchison_distance_df <- dist_calculator(subset_1500, "aitchison" ,metadata_df)
bray_distance_df <-  dist_calculator(rarefied.reads.1500, "bray" ,metadata_df)

bray_distance_df
   

```

```{r}

#same as above but NOT removing duplicates - will be important for stability analyses below 
dist_calculator.all <- function(ps, dist, metadata) {
  
  
  
fixed_taxa <- ps %>%
 tax_fix(unknowns = "NA")

  
 dists <- fixed_taxa %>%
  dist_calc(dist) %>%
   dist_get() 
   
     
dists <- as.matrix(dists)
 
 melted_vOTU <- dists  %>%
   melt()
 
 melted_vOTU
  
 

 colnames(melted_vOTU) <- c("sample1", "sample2", "dist.vOTU")

 
  
 fam <- tax_glom(ps, taxrank="Family", NArm = TRUE) 
fixed_taxa_fam <- fam %>%
 tax_fix(unknowns = "NA")
 fam
 
 
 
 dist_fam <- fam %>%
  dist_calc(dist) %>%
   dist_get() 
 
 
  dist_fam <- as.matrix(dist_fam)

  melted_PBF <- dist_fam  %>%
   melt()
  
  
  
   colnames(melted_PBF) <- c("sample1", "sample2", "dist.pbf")

   
   
   dist.merged.all <- left_join(melted_vOTU %>% select(sample1, sample2, dist.vOTU), 
                         melted_PBF %>% select(sample1, sample2, dist.pbf), 
                         by = c("sample1" = "sample1", "sample2" = "sample2")) %>% 
                       left_join(metadata %>% select(Participant.ID, filename), by = c("sample1" = "filename")) %>% 
                       left_join(metadata %>% select(Participant.ID, filename), by = c("sample2" = "filename")) %>% 
                        melt()


colnames(dist.merged.all) <- c("sample1", "sample2", "subject1", "subject2", "metric", "value")
dist.merged.all

dist.merged.all$metric <- factor(dist.merged.all$metric, levels = c("dist.vOTU", "dist.pbf"))

#Same subject column to allow to dinstinguish between same v same vs. same vs different comparisons. 

dist.merged.all <- dist.merged.all %>% mutate(same.subject = subject1 == subject2)


dist.merged.all
# Remove rows where 'Value' is equal to 0 because this is same v same comparison  
dist.merged.all <- dist.merged.all[dist.merged.all$value != 0, ]
dist.merged.all



#change names 


dist.merged.all$same.subject = as.character(dist.merged.all$same.subject)
dist.merged.all$metric = as.character(dist.merged.all$metric)




dist.merged.all$same.subject[dist.merged.all$same.subject == "TRUE"] = "Intra-Individual"
dist.merged.all$same.subject[dist.merged.all$same.subject == "FALSE"] = "Inter-Individual"


dist.merged.all$metric[dist.merged.all$metric == "dist.vOTU"] = "vOTU"
dist.merged.all$metric[dist.merged.all$metric == "dist.pbf"] = "PBF"

colors <- c("#C3B1E1", "#a0d6b4")



dist.merged.all$metric <- factor(dist.merged.all$metric, levels = c("vOTU", "PBF"))



dist.merged.all

}



```

```{r}
#BRAY 

##FOR the wilcoxon, I'll need to first reshape the data 

bray_distance_df.wider <- bray_distance_df %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = metric, values_from = value)




# Now on just intra -comparisons 
dist.merged.all_intra.bray <- bray_distance_df.wider[bray_distance_df.wider$same.subject == "Intra-Individual",]

#intra wilcox test
wilcox.test(Pair(vOTU, PBF) ~ 1, data = dist.merged.all_intra.bray)


dist.merged.all_inter.bray <- bray_distance_df.wider[bray_distance_df.wider$same.subject == "Inter-Individual",]

#inter wilox test 
wilcox.test(Pair(vOTU, PBF) ~ 1, data = dist.merged.all_inter.bray)


#now plot with significance stars 


#removing the test variable x
rm(x)
colors <- c("#C3B1E1", "#a0d6b4")


#now plot 
 bray_distance_df  %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = same.subject, y = value, fill = metric)) +
 geom_violin(scale = "width", aes(fill=metric), alpha = 0.3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.3, aes(fill=metric), color="black", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
    annotate("segment", x = 0.75, xend = 1.25, y = 1.09, yend = 1.09, 
           linetype = "solid", color = "black", size = 1) +
     annotate("text", x = 1, y = 1.11, label = "****", size = 6, color = "black") +  

       annotate("segment", x = 1.75, xend = 2.25, y = 1.03, yend = 1.03, 
           linetype = "solid", color = "black", size = 1) +
     annotate("text", x = 2, y = 1.05, label = "****", size = 6, color = "black") +  

  scale_fill_viridis(discrete = T, begin = 0.1, end = 0.7, labels = labels) +
  scale_colour_viridis(discrete = T, begin = 0.1, end = 0.7, labels = labels) +
 # geom_jitter(alpha = 0.05) +
  #scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") + 
  #facet_wrap(. ~ same.subject, labeller = label_parsed) + 
   scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +  
  scale_color_manual(values = colors) +  
   labs(x = "Comparison", y = "Distance") +
   labs(fill = "Distance metric") + 
   scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Inter-individual (across)", "Intra-individual (within)")) +
  theme_bw() 

```


```{r}
#Aitchison's

  pivot_wider(names_from = metric, values_from = value)




# Now on just intra -comparisons

#inter wilox test

#now plot with significance stars

rm(x)

#now plot 
 aitchison_distance_df  %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = same.subject, y = value, fill = metric)) +
 geom_violin(scale = "width", aes(fill=metric), alpha = 0.3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.3, aes(fill=metric), color="black", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
    annotate("segment", x = 0.75, xend = 1.25, y = 105, yend = 105, 
           linetype = "solid", color = "black", size = 1) +
     annotate("text", x = 1, y = 108, label = "****", size = 6, color = "black") +  

       annotate("segment", x = 1.75, xend = 2.25, y = 93, yend = 93, 
           linetype = "solid", color = "black", size = 1) +
     annotate("text", x = 2, y = 96, label = "****", size = 6, color = "black") +  

  scale_fill_viridis(discrete = T, begin = 0.1, end = 0.7, labels = labels) +
  scale_colour_viridis(discrete = T, begin = 0.1, end = 0.7, labels = labels) +
 # geom_jitter(alpha = 0.05) +
  #scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") + 
  #facet_wrap(. ~ same.subject, labeller = label_parsed) + 
   scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +  
  scale_color_manual(values = colors) +  
   labs(x = "Comparison", y = "Distance") +
   labs(fill = "Distance metric") + 
   scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Inter-individual (across)", "Intra-individual (within)")) +
  theme_bw() 

```

```{r}
###CALCULATE STABILITY ####

#need the distance df (without duplicates removed - this will happen as I set sample2>sample1 )


bray_distance_df.all <-  dist_calculator.all(rarefied.reads.1500, "bray" ,metadata_df)


#add a metadata column for "time point " - converting week number to time point (ie- week 0,2,9 = 1,2,3)

test_metadata <- hmp2_metadata_phylo
test_metadata <- test_metadata[order(test_metadata$Participant.ID, test_metadata$week_num), ]
#ordering by week number for each individual 
#then creating a sequence for the number of time points per individual 
test_metadata$time_point <- sequence(rle(test_metadata$Participant.ID)$lengths)
write.csv(test_metadata, "/Users/anshulsinha/Desktop/test/test_meta.csv")



#Filter for just intra comparisons

dist.merged.all.filt.timepoints <- bray_distance_df.all %>%
  #Filter for just intra comparisons
  filter(same.subject == "Intra-Individual") %>%
  #unclear what this line is doing- ask Michael
 # filter(!metric %in% c("dist.aitch.filt", "dist.aitch.filt.pbf")) %>%
  select(sample1, sample2, metric, value) %>%
  left_join(test_metadata %>% select(time_point, filename, Participant.ID), by = c("sample1" = "filename")) %>%
  left_join(test_metadata %>% select(time_point, filename, Participant.ID), by = c("sample2" = "filename"), suffix = c(".sample1", ".sample2")) %>%
  mutate()

length(unique(bray_distance_df$sample1))

length(unique(dist.merged.all.filt.timepoints$sample2))




library(dplyr)

dist.merged.all.filt.timepoints.2 <- dist.merged.all.filt.timepoints %>%
  mutate(timepoint_after = time_point.sample2 > time_point.sample1) %>%
  filter(timepoint_after) %>%
  mutate(timepoint_diff = time_point.sample2 - time_point.sample1) %>%
  group_by(Participant.ID.sample1, metric, time_point.sample1) %>%
  arrange(timepoint_diff) %>%
 filter(timepoint_diff == min(timepoint_diff))   # Keep only the row with the minimum timepoint_diff per group

dist.merged.all.filt.timepoints.2

#Within each time_point.sample1 (group by patient and metric - this will take only the min timepoint_diff per group - allows us to sample "consecutive"time points )

#note that there are several cases where the min_timepoint_diff is > 1 
#some reasons? 
  #we filtered out some samples from 






test_metadata <- hmp2_metadata_phylo
test_metadata <- test_metadata %>%
  select(Participant.ID, diagnosis.x)


colnames(test_metadata) <- c("Participant.ID.sample1", "Diagnosis")


dist.merged.all.filt.timepoints.final_meta <- left_join(dist.merged.all.filt.timepoints.2, test_metadata, by= "Participant.ID.sample1")

dist.merged.all.filt.timepoints.final_meta <- unique(dist.merged.all.filt.timepoints.final_meta)


dist.merged.all.filt.timepoints.final_meta %>%
  filter(!metric %in% c("vOTU")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Participant.ID.sample1, y = value, group = interaction(Participant.ID.sample1, metric), color = metric)) +
    geom_violin(scale = "width", aes(fill=metric), alpha = 0.3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.3, aes(fill = metric), color = "black", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
    scale_fill_viridis(discrete = T, begin = 0.1, end = 0.3, labels = labels) +
    scale_colour_viridis(discrete = T, begin = 0.1, end = 0.3, labels = labels) +
    theme_bw() +
    labs(x = "Individual", y = "Stability\n(1 - distance)", color = "Distance metric", fill = "Distance metric") +
    facet_wrap(. ~ Diagnosis)







dist.merged.all.filt.timepoints.final_meta %>%
  filter(!metric %in% c("PBF")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Participant.ID.sample1, y = value, group = interaction(Participant.ID.sample1, metric), color = metric)) +
  geom_violin(scale = "width", aes(fill=metric), alpha = 0.3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.3, aes(fill = metric), color = "black", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
    scale_fill_viridis(discrete = T, begin = 0.1, end = 0.3, labels = labels) +
    scale_colour_viridis(discrete = T, begin = 0.1, end = 0.3, labels = labels) +
    theme_bw() +
    labs(x = "Individual", y = "Stability\n(1 - distance)", color = "Distance metric", fill = "Distance metric") +
     facet_wrap(. ~ Diagnosis)




#Filter for just PBF

dist.merged.all.filt.timepoints.final_meta_PBF <- dist.merged.all.filt.timepoints.final_meta %>%
  filter(!metric %in% c("vOTU"))


#Filter for just vOTU


dist.merged.all.filt.timepoints.final_meta_votu <- dist.merged.all.filt.timepoints.final_meta %>%
  filter(!metric %in% c("PBF"))


dist.merged.all.filt.timepoints.final_meta_agg_vOTU <-  aggregate(value ~ Diagnosis, data = dist.merged.all.filt.timepoints.final_meta_votu, FUN = mean)
dist.merged.all.filt.timepoints.final_meta_agg_PBF <-  aggregate(value ~ Diagnosis, data = dist.merged.all.filt.timepoints.final_meta_PBF, FUN = mean)



#Filter for just vOTU
#calculate the Mean value for each individual at the PBF level


PBF_grouped <- dist.merged.all.filt.timepoints.final_meta_PBF %>%
group_by(Participant.ID.sample1) %>%
    # Calculate the mean of the "Frequency" column for each group
    summarise_at(vars(value),
                 list(mean_values = mean))


unique_metadata <- unique(test_metadata)
unique_metadata <- unique_metadata %>%
  select(Participant.ID.sample1
, Diagnosis)


PBF_grouped <- left_join(PBF_grouped, unique_metadata, by= "Participant.ID.sample1")



votu_grouped <- dist.merged.all.filt.timepoints.final_meta_votu %>%
group_by(Participant.ID.sample1) %>%
    # Calculate the mean of the "Frequency" column for each group
    summarise_at(vars(value),
                 list(mean_values = mean))


votu_grouped <- left_join(votu_grouped, unique_metadata, by= "Participant.ID.sample1")

#write.csv(PBF_grouped, "/Users/anshulsinha/Desktop/IBD_prophages_aim/bioinformatics_aim/bioinformatics_HMP_2/free_virome/free_virome_09_23/diversity_analyses/whole_virome_PBF_instability.csv")
mean_vals <-  aggregate(mean_values ~ Diagnosis, data = PBF_grouped, FUN = mean)


#PLOT




votu_grouped %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Diagnosis, y = mean_values, color = Diagnosis)) +
      geom_boxplot(width = 0.5, colour = "grey35", outlier.shape = NA) +
      geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5, aes(color = Diagnosis)) +
    theme_bw() +
    labs(x = "Individual", y = "Bray Distance (From Previous Time Point)", color = "Diagnosis Status", fill = "Diagnosis Status") +
      scale_color_manual(values = diagnosis_cols)



PBF_grouped %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Diagnosis, y = mean_values, color = Diagnosis)) +
      geom_boxplot(width = 0.5, colour = "grey35", outlier.shape = NA) +
      geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5, aes(color = Diagnosis)) +
    theme_bw() +
    labs(x = "Individual", y = "Bray Distance (From Previous Time Point)", color = "Diagnosis Status", fill = "Diagnosis Status") +
      scale_color_manual(values = diagnosis_cols)


kruskal.test(mean_values ~ Diagnosis, data = votu_grouped)
kruskal.test(mean_values ~ Diagnosis, data = PBF_grouped)

pairwise.wilcox.test(votu_grouped$mean_values, votu_grouped$Diagnosis,
                 p.adjust.method = "BH")

pairwise.wilcox.test(PBF_grouped$mean_values, PBF_grouped$Diagnosis,
                 p.adjust.method = "BH")
```

```{r}
#calculate vOTU and PBF prevalence 
#NOT USING PHYLOSEQ OBJECT - TAKING ADVANTAGE OF STOCKDALE CUTOFFS FROM OTU_TABLE - PS introduces zeroes 


#filter for the samples with more than 1500 counts 

#get names of those samples 

filtered.1500.sampledata <-  data.frame(sample_data(subset_1500))

sample.names.1500 <- filtered.1500.sampledata$filename

#filter df_hmp_virome for those counts 
filtered.1500.df_hmp_virome <- df_hmp_virome %>%
  filter(filename %in% sample.names.1500)

length(unique(filtered.1500.df_hmp_virome$filename)) #sanity check -1093 filenames 

length(unique(filtered.1500.df_hmp_virome$Participant.ID)) #sanity check -115 filenames 


length(unique(filtered.1500.df_hmp_virome$contigname)) #3,870 contigs total in filtered 1500
length(unique(df_hmp_virome$contigname)) #3,870 contigs total in filtered 1500


nrow(filtered.1500.df_hmp_virome) # 471076
nrow(distinct(filtered.1500.df_hmp_virome, filename, contigname)) #471076


#merged with iphop
df_hmp_virome_merged <- left_join(filtered.1500.df_hmp_virome, hmp2_vir_iphop, by="contigname")



nrow(df_hmp_virome_merged) # 471076
nrow(distinct(df_hmp_virome_merged, filename, contigname)) #471076


df_hmp_virome_merged
nrow(distinct(filtered.1500.df_hmp_virome, filename, contigname))





#using the cutoff of >1 mean depth in addition to only keeping samples > 1500 reads, we lose a few contigs  
#>1 is a bit more strict- rather than in the ps object which could round from 0.5-0.99 up to 1
#More conservative is better for prevalence info in this case 
 #471,076 
#3886 vs. 3862
df_hmp_virome_merged <- df_hmp_virome_merged[df_hmp_virome_merged$meandepth > 1,]




 length(unique(df_hmp_virome$contigname)) - length(unique(df_hmp_virome_merged$contigname)) 

 #we only lose 24 - would've expected a bit more 
 
 
 #quick check how many of these are NAs
 df_hmp_virome_merged.dup <- df_hmp_virome_merged[!duplicated(df_hmp_virome_merged$contigname),]
 
 table(df_hmp_virome_merged.dup$Family, useNA = "always")
 
 
 1 - (497/3862)
 3862-497

 length(unique(df_hmp_virome_merged$Family, useNA = "never")) 
 
#Sanity check
 df_hmp_virome_merged_test2 <- df_hmp_virome_merged[df_hmp_virome_merged$filename == "C3001_12_CD_P",]
  
 
df_hmp_virome_merged_sanity_rum <- df_hmp_virome_merged[df_hmp_virome_merged$Family == "f__Ruminococcaceae",]
  

length(unique(df_hmp_virome_merged_sanity_rum$filename))
  length(unique(df_hmp_virome_merged_sanity_rum$Participant.ID))

#prevalence at the vOTU level 
 #note here that Im calculating based on prevalence at the SAMPLE and the INDIVIDUAL level 
 
 
df_hmp_virome_votu_prevalence <- df_hmp_virome_merged %>%
  group_by(contigname) %>%
  summarize(sample_count = n_distinct(filename), prevalence = n_distinct(filename) / n_distinct(df_hmp_virome_merged$filename),
            participant_count = n_distinct(Participant.ID), prevalence_ind = n_distinct(Participant.ID)/ n_distinct(df_hmp_virome_merged$Participant.ID))





#add a rank column 
df_hmp_virome_votu_prevalence$rank_prev <- rank(-df_hmp_virome_votu_prevalence$prevalence)
df_hmp_virome_votu_prevalence$rank_prev_ind <- rank(-df_hmp_virome_votu_prevalence$prevalence_ind)

#prevalence at the PBF level 
df_hmp_virome_pbf_prevalence <- df_hmp_virome_merged %>%
  group_by(Family) %>%
  summarize(sample_count = n_distinct(filename), prevalence = n_distinct(filename)/ n_distinct(df_hmp_virome_merged$filename),
            participant_count = n_distinct(Participant.ID), prevalence_ind = n_distinct(Participant.ID)/ n_distinct(df_hmp_virome_merged$Participant.ID))




#collapse Family NAs as unidentified and then remove 
df_hmp_virome_pbf_prevalence <- df_hmp_virome_pbf_prevalence %>%
  mutate(Family = ifelse(is.na(Family), "unidentified", Family)) %>% 
  filter(Family != "unidentified")

df_hmp_virome_pbf_prevalence



#add a rank column in descending order 
df_hmp_virome_pbf_prevalence$rank_prev <- rank(-df_hmp_virome_pbf_prevalence$prevalence)
df_hmp_virome_pbf_prevalence$rank_prev_ind <- rank(-df_hmp_virome_pbf_prevalence$prevalence_ind)

#now group by filename and family so that meandepth per Family is calculated

library(dplyr)

df_hmp_virome_merged.pbf.1 <- df_hmp_virome_merged %>%
  mutate(
    Family = ifelse(is.na(Family), "unidentified", Family)
  ) %>%
  group_by(filename) %>%
  mutate(
    sum_depth = sum(meandepth)              # total depth per sample
  ) %>%
  group_by(filename, Family) %>%
  summarise(
    sum_depth = dplyr::first(sum_depth),    # same for all rows in this group
    fam_depth = sum(meandepth),             # depth for this Family in this sample
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r}
 min(sample_sums(subset_1500_tax_glom_mas))

 
###Filter out low prevlance PBFs - If

 #first melt the PS to determine the OTUs that correspond to the PBFs
ps_melted_filtered_maslin <- ps_melt(subset_1500_tax_glom_mas)

length(unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin$OTU)) #74 left 
length(unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin$Family)) #74 left 

#now take the contignames that are associated with the families that I want to keep since PS agglomerates to an arbitrary contigname per family 

ps_melted_filtered_maslin_PHFs_prev <- ps_melted_filtered_maslin %>% 
  filter(Family %in% PBF_list_50_prev$Family )

length(unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin_PHFs_prev$Family)) #18 in here 
length(unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin_PHFs_prev$OTU)) #18 in here 

#now take the OTU names from this 
 keepTaxa_mas_whole_vir = unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin_PHFs_prev$OTU)



tax_glom_fam_pruned <- prune_taxa(keepTaxa_mas_whole_vir, subset_1500_tax_glom_mas)
#18 families 
 min(sample_sums(tax_glom_fam_pruned))
 sort(sample_sums(tax_glom_fam_pruned)) # a few low samples, but overall not bad 
  sort(sample_sums(subset_1500_tax_glom_mas)) # a few low samples, but overall not bad 

  
  before_counts <- sample_sums(subset_1500_tax_glom_mas)
after_counts  <- sample_sums(tax_glom_fam_pruned)

df <- data.frame(
  sample = names(before_counts),
  before = as.numeric(before_counts),
  after  = as.numeric(after_counts)
)

df$percent_retained <- (df$after / df$before) * 100

 
###Went from 74 to 18 taxa 


#now also remove samples that have zero counts 



phyloseq_richness_filter <- function(physeq, mintaxa = 10){
  sp <- estimate_richness(physeq, measures = "Observed")
  samples_to_keep <- rownames(sp)[ which(sp$Observed >= mintaxa) ]
  
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) == 0){
    stop("All samples will be removed.\n")  
  }
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) == nsamples(physeq)){
    cat("All samples will be preserved\n")
    res <- physeq
  }
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) < nsamples(physeq)){
    res <- prune_samples(samples = samples_to_keep, x = physeq)
  }
 
  return(res)
}

tax_glom_maslin_filtered_richness <- phyloseq_richness_filter(tax_glom_fam_pruned, mintaxa = 1)

#now at 1092 samples

length(unique(meta_maaslin_whole_vir$Participant.ID))

#make sample the rownames
maslin_otu_pivot_whole_vir <- as.data.frame(maslin_otu_pivot_whole_vir)
rownames(maslin_otu_pivot_whole_vir) <- maslin_otu_pivot_whole_vir$Sample
maslin_otu_pivot_whole_vir$Sample <- NULL

maslin_otu_pivot_whole_vir

hmp2_vir_iphop_subset_whole_vir$Family

                          hmp2_vir_iphop_subset_whole_vir$contigname)



#Takes the column names from maslin_otu_pivot_whole_vir (each  a contig/OTU ID). 
#Uses those as indices in otu_to_family_whole_vir to look up the family name for each contig.
#Assigns those family names as the new column names of your pivot table.
maslin_otu_pivot_whole_vir_fam_names <- maslin_otu_pivot_whole_vir
colnames(maslin_otu_pivot_whole_vir_fam_names) <- otu_to_family_whole_vir[colnames(maslin_otu_pivot_whole_vir_fam_names)]

maslin_otu_pivot_whole_vir
hmp2_vir_iphop_subset_whole_vir

all(rownames(maslin_otu_pivot_whole_vir_fam_names) == rownames(maslin_meta_whole_vir))
#False but thats ok because maaslin will change order automatically
```

```{r}
ps_rel <- transform_sample_counts(subset_1500.temp, function(x) x / sum(x))

# 2. Then subset to Enterobacteriaceae
ps_entero_rel <- subset_taxa(ps_rel, Family == "f__Enterobacteriaceae")

# 3. Melt, aggregate, and remove zeroes
df_entero_fam <- psmelt(ps_entero_rel) %>%
  group_by(Sample, dysbiosis_binary) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0)  # remove zeroes

# 4. Plot on log scale
ggplot(df_entero_fam, aes(x = dysbiosis_binary, y = Abundance, fill = dysbiosis_binary)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.85, color = "black", size = 1.2) +
  geom_jitter(color = "black", width = 0.2, size = 4, alpha = 0.85) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Relative Abundance (log10)", title = "f__Enterobacteriaceae") +
  guides(fill = "none") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 22) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black")
  )
```

```{r}
#Temperate virome here:

#dysbiotic vs. non-dysbiotic 

#PS object of samples with more than 1500 reads -

  #but note that this is not filtered for > 700. Will do both and perform comparison



#1) tax glom- removing NAs - dont really want to include unknowns or consider single contigs as families here


 subset_1500_tax_glom_mas_temp1 <- tax_glom(subset_1500.temp, taxrank="Family", NArm = TRUE) 
#53 taxa left from 2,176 temperate vOTUs

 sample_sums(subset_1500_tax_glom_mas_temp1)
 
 
###Filter out low prevlance PBFs - If
##Choosing arbitrarily that it must be found in at least 50 % of individuals per above prevalence analyses  - will possibly improve power - and will help us deal with sparsity 

 #first melt the PS to determine the OTUs that correspond to the PBFs
ps_melted_filtered_maslin_temp1 <- ps_melt(subset_1500_tax_glom_mas_temp1)



length(unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin_temp1$OTU)) #53 left 
length(unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin_temp1$Family)) #53 left 

#now take the contignames that are associated with the families that I want to keep since PS agglomerates to an arbitrary contigname per family 

ps_melted_filtered_maslin_PHFs_prev_temp1 <- ps_melted_filtered_maslin_temp1 %>% 
  filter(Family %in% PBF_list_50_prev$Family )

length(unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin_PHFs_prev_temp1$Family)) #18 in here 
length(unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin_PHFs_prev_temp1$OTU)) #18 in here 

#now take the vOTU names from this 
 keepTaxa_mas_temp1 = unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin_PHFs_prev_temp1$OTU)


 
 
 
#Now prune the taxa to only keep the 18 most prevalent families
 
tax_glom_fam_pruned_temp1 <- prune_taxa(keepTaxa_mas_temp1, subset_1500_tax_glom_mas_temp1)
#we jave the the 18 most prevalent again 

#18 families 
 min(sample_sums(tax_glom_fam_pruned_temp1))
 sort(sample_sums(tax_glom_fam_pruned_temp1)) # a few low samples, but overall not bad 
#most above 400 at least 
 
 


 #Sanity: would filtering directly by family give me the same results as doing it by OTU?
 
 families_to_keep_test <- PBF_list_50_prev$Family

taxa_to_keep_test <- taxa_names(subset_1500_tax_glom_mas_temp1)[
  tax_table(subset_1500_tax_glom_mas_temp1)[, "Family"] %in% families_to_keep_test
]

tax_glom_fam_pruned_temp1_test <- prune_taxa(
  taxa_to_keep_test, 
  subset_1500_tax_glom_mas_temp1
)
 
# Get abundance matrices
mat1 <- as.data.frame(otu_table(tax_glom_fam_pruned_temp1))
mat2 <- as.data.frame(otu_table(tax_glom_fam_pruned_temp1_test))  # your "original" method

# Ensure same ordering
mat1 <- mat1[order(rownames(mat1)), order(colnames(mat1))]
mat2 <- mat2[order(rownames(mat2)), order(colnames(mat2))]

# Check equality
all.equal(mat1, mat2)
 
 
 fam1 <- sort(unique(tax_table(tax_glom_fam_pruned_temp1)[, "Family"]))
fam2 <- sort(unique(tax_table(tax_glom_fam_pruned_temp1_test)[, "Family"]))

identical(fam1, fam2)
 

 setequal(
  taxa_names(tax_glom_fam_pruned_temp1),
  taxa_names(tax_glom_fam_pruned_temp1_test)
)
 
 
 
#now also remove samples that have zero counts 



phyloseq_richness_filter <- function(physeq, mintaxa = 10){
  sp <- estimate_richness(physeq, measures = "Observed")
  samples_to_keep <- rownames(sp)[ which(sp$Observed >= mintaxa) ]
  
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) == 0){
    stop("All samples will be removed.\n")  
  }
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) == nsamples(physeq)){
    cat("All samples will be preserved\n")
    res <- physeq
  }
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) < nsamples(physeq)){
    res <- prune_samples(samples = samples_to_keep, x = physeq)
  }
 
  return(res)
}

tax_glom_maslin_filtered_richness_temp1 <- phyloseq_richness_filter(tax_glom_fam_pruned_temp1, mintaxa = 1)
```

```{r}
PBF_list_50_prev$Family
physeqhmp2tempvir

# Convert to relative abundance
ps_rel <- transform_sample_counts(physeqhmp2tempvir, function(x) x / sum(x))

# Subset to sample of interest
ps_sample <- subset_samples(ps_rel, sample_names(ps_rel) == "E5004_24_UC")

# Melt and aggregate at family level
psmelt(ps_sample) %>%
  group_by(Family) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  arrange(desc(Abundance))
```

```{r}
#filter for dysbiosis 
results_temperatefit_allsamplesdysb <- results_temperatefit_allsamples[results_temperatefit_allsamples$metadata == "dysbiosis_binary",]

#Filter for Abundance 
results_temperatefit_allsamplesdysb_ab <- results_temperatefit_allsamplesdysb[results_temperatefit_allsamplesdysb$model == "abundance",]


results_temperatefit_allsamplesdysb_ab_filter <- results_temperatefit_allsamplesdysb_ab %>% 
  mutate(log10_qval = -log10(qval_individual),
    significance_direction = case_when(
      qval_individual <= 0.1 & coef >= 1 ~ "Enriched in Dysbiotic Samples",
      qval_individual <= 0.1 & coef <= -1 ~ "Enriched in Non-Dysbiotic Samples",
      TRUE ~ "Not Significant"
    ))


#
library(ggrepel)
ggplot(results_temperatefit_allsamplesdysb_ab_filter,
       aes(x = coef, y = log10_qval, color = significance_direction)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 1.5, size = 3.5) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Enriched in Dysbiotic Samples"     = "forestgreen",
      "Enriched in Non-Dysbiotic Samples" = "#D4AF37",
      "Not Significant"                   = "gray"
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Temperate Virome",
    x = "Coefficient",
    y = "-log10(p-value)",
    color = "Dysbiosis Status"
  ) +
  
  # dashed threshold lines
  geom_vline(xintercept = -1, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1,  linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1,  linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  
  theme(
    plot.title     = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16),
    axis.title.x   = element_text(size = 19),
    axis.title.y   = element_text(size = 19),
    legend.title   = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text    = element_text(size = 17),
    legend.position = "right",
     axis.text.x    = element_text(size = 18),   # <–– added
  axis.text.y    = element_text(size = 18),   # <–– added
    
    # remove gridlines
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    
    # **solid x and y axes**
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.8),
    
    # add border around plotting area (optional but looks clean)
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.8)
  )

results_temperatefit_allsamplesdysb_ab_filter
```

```{r}
### MAASLIN3 #### FOR DYSBIOTIC SAMPLES 
#Virulent virome here:

#dysbiotic vs. non-dysbiotic 

#PS object of samples with more than 1500 reads - 

subset_1500.vir <-  subset_samples(physeqhmp2vir, length_normalized_read_count > 1500)


subset_1500.virulent.fam.glom.first <- tax_glom(subset_1500.vir, taxrank="Family", NArm = TRUE) 




#1) tax glom- removing NAs - dont really want to include unknowns or single contigs here
#62 taxa left from 1,710 virulent vOTUs

###Filter out low prevlance PBFs - If
##Choosing arbitrarily that it must be found in at least 50 % of individuals per above prevalence analyses  - 

 #first melt the PS to determine the OTUs that correspond to the PBFs
ps_melted_filtered_maslin_vir1 <- ps_melt(subset_1500.virulent.fam.glom.first)



length(unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin_vir1$OTU)) #62 left 
length(unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin_vir1$Family)) #62 left 

#now take the contignames that are associated with the families that I want to keep since PS agglomerates to an arbitrary contigname per family 

ps_melted_filtered_maslin_PHFs_prev_vir1 <- ps_melted_filtered_maslin_vir1 %>% 
  filter(Family %in% PBF_list_50_prev$Family )

length(unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin_PHFs_prev_vir1$Family)) #18 in here 
length(unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin_PHFs_prev_vir1$OTU)) #18 in here 

#now take the vOTU names from this 
 keepTaxa_mas_vir1 = unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin_PHFs_prev_vir1$OTU)


#Now prune the taxa to only keep the 18 most prevalent families
 
tax_glom_fam_pruned_vir <- prune_taxa(keepTaxa_mas_vir1, subset_1500.virulent.fam.glom.first)
#we jave the the 18 most prevalent again 

#18 families 
 min(sample_sums(tax_glom_fam_pruned_vir))
 sort(sample_sums(tax_glom_fam_pruned_vir)) # a few low samples, but overall not bad 
#most above 400 at least 
  sort(sample_sums(subset_1500.virulent.fam.glom.first)) # a few low samples, but overall not bad 


 
 


#now also remove samples that have zero counts 



phyloseq_richness_filter <- function(physeq, mintaxa = 10){
  sp <- estimate_richness(physeq, measures = "Observed")
  samples_to_keep <- rownames(sp)[ which(sp$Observed >= mintaxa) ]
  
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) == 0){
    stop("All samples will be removed.\n")  
  }
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) == nsamples(physeq)){
    cat("All samples will be preserved\n")
    res <- physeq
  }
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) < nsamples(physeq)){
    res <- prune_samples(samples = samples_to_keep, x = physeq)
  }
 
  return(res)
}

tax_glom_maslin_filtered_richness_vir1 <- phyloseq_richness_filter(tax_glom_fam_pruned_vir, mintaxa = 1)

                          hmp2_vir_iphop_subset_vir1$contigname)



#Takes the column names from maslin_otu_pivot_whole_vir (each  a contig/OTU ID). 
#Uses those as indices in otu_to_family_whole_vir to look up the family name for each contig.
#Assigns those family names as the new column names of your pivot table.
maslin_otu_pivot_virvir_fam_names_vir1 <- maslin_otu_pivot_vir1
colnames(maslin_otu_pivot_virvir_fam_names_vir1) <- otu_to_family_vir1[colnames(maslin_otu_pivot_virvir_fam_names_vir1)]
```

```{r}
###VOLCANO### from final version

results_virfit_allsamples <- read.table("/Users/anshulsinha/Desktop/IBD_prophages_aim/bioinformatics_aim/bioinformatics_HMP_2/maaslin3_11_2025/virulent_virome_CDUC/dysbiosis_output/all_results.tsv", header=TRUE)


#filter for dysbiosis 
results_virfit_allsamples_dysb <- results_virfit_allsamples[results_virfit_allsamples$metadata == "dysbiosis_binary",]

#Filter for Abundance 
results_virfit_allsamples_dysb_ab <- results_virfit_allsamples_dysb[results_virfit_allsamples_dysb$model == "abundance",]


results_virfit_allsamples_dysb_ab_filter <- results_virfit_allsamples_dysb_ab %>% 
  mutate(log10_qval = -log10(qval_individual),
    significance_direction = case_when(
      qval_individual <= 0.1 & coef >= 1 ~ "Enriched in Dysbiotic Samples",
      qval_individual <= 0.1 & coef <= -1 ~ "Enriched in Non-Dysbiotic Samples",
      TRUE ~ "Not Significant"
    ))


#Poor fitting of Mariniflaceae - remove NA 

results_virfit_allsamples_dysb_ab_filter <- results_virfit_allsamples_dysb_ab_filter[!is.na(results_virfit_allsamples_dysb_ab_filter$coef), ]

####Will use this for the manuscript - 12-2025 #####


library(ggrepel)
ggplot(results_virfit_allsamples_dysb_ab_filter,
       aes(x = coef, y = log10_qval, color = significance_direction)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 1.5, size = 3.5) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Enriched in Dysbiotic Samples"     = "forestgreen",
      "Enriched in Non-Dysbiotic Samples" = "#D4AF37",
      "Not Significant"                   = "gray"
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Virulent Virome",
    x = "Coefficient",
    y = "-log10(p-value)",
    color = "Dysbiosis Status"
  ) +
  
  # dashed threshold lines
  geom_vline(xintercept = -1, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1,  linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1,  linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  
  theme(
    plot.title     = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16),
    axis.title.x   = element_text(size = 19),
    axis.title.y   = element_text(size = 19),
    legend.title   = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text    = element_text(size = 17),
    legend.position = "right",
      axis.text.x    = element_text(size = 18),   # <–– added
  axis.text.y    = element_text(size = 18),   # <–– added
    # remove gridlines
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    
    # **solid x and y axes**
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.8),
    
    # add border around plotting area (optional but looks clean)
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.8)
  ) +
    xlim(-2, 2) 





library(ggrepel)
```

```{r}
length(unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin_PHFs_prev_vir1.2$Family))

  filter(Family %in% PBF_list_50_prev.2$Family )

length(unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin_PHFs_prev_vir1.2$Family)) #18 in here

 keepTaxa_mas_vir1.2 = unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin_PHFs_prev_vir1.2$OTU)

#most above 400 at least 
  sort(sample_sums(tax_glom_fam_pruned_vir.2)) # a few low samples, but overall not bad 

 
 
 


#now also remove samples that have zero counts 



phyloseq_richness_filter <- function(physeq, mintaxa = 10){
  sp <- estimate_richness(physeq, measures = "Observed")
  samples_to_keep <- rownames(sp)[ which(sp$Observed >= mintaxa) ]
  
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) == 0){
    stop("All samples will be removed.\n")  
  }
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) == nsamples(physeq)){
    cat("All samples will be preserved\n")
    res <- physeq
  }
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) < nsamples(physeq)){
    res <- prune_samples(samples = samples_to_keep, x = physeq)
  }
 
  return(res)
}

tax_glom_maslin_filtered_richness_vir1.2 <- phyloseq_richness_filter(tax_glom_fam_pruned_vir.2, mintaxa = 1)

#now at 1092 samples 
sort(sample_sums(tax_glom_maslin_filtered_richness_vir1.2))
max(sample_sums(tax_glom_maslin_filtered_richness_vir1.2))

meta_maaslin_vir1_melt.2 <- ps_melt(tax_glom_maslin_filtered_richness_vir1.2)
length(unique(meta_maaslin_vir1.2$Participant.ID))

melted_maslin1_vir1.2 <- psmelt(otu_table(tax_glom_maslin_filtered_richness_vir1.2))
#use tiydrpivot wider to format into a useable matrix 

length(unique(melted_maslin1_vir1.2$OTU))

maslin_otu_pivot_vir1.2 <-  tidyr::pivot_wider(melted_maslin1_vir1.2, names_from = OTU, values_from = Abundance)

#sanity that metling works:

#make sample the rownames
maslin_otu_pivot_vir1.2 <- as.data.frame(maslin_otu_pivot_vir1.2)
rownames(maslin_otu_pivot_vir1.2) <- maslin_otu_pivot_vir1.2$Sample
maslin_otu_pivot_vir1.2$Sample <- NULL




#now extract the metadata 

maslin_meta_vir1.2 <- sample_data(tax_glom_maslin_filtered_richness_vir1.2)

#now I want to change the names of the OTUs to family 
col_names_vir1.2 <- colnames(maslin_otu_pivot_vir1.2)

#from the initial iphop... subset to only contain those OTU names 
hmp2_vir_iphop_subset_vir1.2 <- hmp2_vir_iphop[hmp2_vir_iphop$contigname %in% col_names_vir1.2, ]
hmp2_vir_iphop_subset_vir1.2$Family

otu_to_family_vir1.2 <- setNames(hmp2_vir_iphop_subset_vir1.2$Family,
                          hmp2_vir_iphop_subset_vir1.2$contigname)
#add the unknown in 
otu_to_family_vir1.2["C3030_k119_44190_flag=1_multi=6.7347_len=3738"] <- "Unknown"


#Takes the column names from maslin_otu_pivot_whole_vir (each  a contig/OTU ID). 
#Uses those as indices in otu_to_family_whole_vir to look up the family name for each contig.
#Assigns those family names as the new column names of your pivot table.
maslin_otu_pivot_virvir_fam_names_vir1.2 <- maslin_otu_pivot_vir1.2
colnames(maslin_otu_pivot_virvir_fam_names_vir1.2) <- otu_to_family_vir1.2[colnames(maslin_otu_pivot_virvir_fam_names_vir1.2)]


#sanity check to confirm switch of OTU names to family names - compare below 

#check if rownames match (this doesnt check order)
setequal(rownames(maslin_otu_pivot_virvir_fam_names_vir1.2), rownames(meta_maaslin_melt_vir1.2))

#False but thats ok because maaslin will change order automatically 


vir_fam_names_abundancedf_vir1.2 <- as.data.frame(maslin_otu_pivot_virvir_fam_names_vir1.2)

maslin_meta_vir1.2 <- data.frame(maslin_meta_vir1.2)

maslin_meta_vir1.2$dysbiosis_binary <- 
  factor(maslin_meta_vir1.2$dysbiosis_binary, levels = c('No', 'Yes'))
maslin_meta_vir1.2$antibiotics <- 
  factor(maslin_meta_vir1.2$antibiotics, levels = c('No', 'Yes'))
```

```{r}
phyloseq_richness_filter <- function(physeq, mintaxa = 10){
  sp <- estimate_richness(physeq, measures = "Observed")
  samples_to_keep <- rownames(sp)[ which(sp$Observed >= mintaxa) ]
  
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) == 0){
    stop("All samples will be removed.\n")  
  }
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) == nsamples(physeq)){
    cat("All samples will be preserved\n")
    res <- physeq
  }
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) < nsamples(physeq)){
    res <- prune_samples(samples = samples_to_keep, x = physeq)
  }
 
  return(res)
}

#add the unknown in
```

```{r}
length(unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin_PHFs_prev_vir1.2$Family))

  filter(Family %in% PBF_list_50_prev.2$Family )

length(unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin_PHFs_prev_vir1.2$Family)) #18 in here 
length(unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin_PHFs_prev_vir1.2$OTU)) #18 in here 

#now take the vOTU names from this 
 keepTaxa_mas_vir1.2 = unique(ps_melted_filtered_maslin_PHFs_prev_vir1.2$OTU)
 
 #Now add the NA vOTU: C3030_k119_44190_flag=1_multi=6.7347_len=3738	
keepTaxa_mas_vir1.2 <- c(keepTaxa_mas_vir1.2, "C3030_k119_44190_flag=1_multi=6.7347_len=3738")
length(keepTaxa_mas_vir1.2) # 19 now 

#Now prune the taxa to only keep the 18 most prevalent families + NA 
 
tax_glom_fam_pruned_vir.2 <- prune_taxa(keepTaxa_mas_vir1.2, subset_1500.virulent.fam.glom.first.2)
#we jave the the 18 most prevalent again 

#18 families  + NA 
 min(sample_sums(tax_glom_fam_pruned_vir.2))
 sort(sample_sums(tax_glom_fam_pruned_vir.2)) # a few low samples, but overall not bad 
#most above 400 at least 
  sort(sample_sums(tax_glom_fam_pruned_vir.2)) # a few low samples, but overall not bad 

 
 
 


#now also remove samples that have zero counts 



phyloseq_richness_filter <- function(physeq, mintaxa = 10){
  sp <- estimate_richness(physeq, measures = "Observed")
  samples_to_keep <- rownames(sp)[ which(sp$Observed >= mintaxa) ]
  
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) == 0){
    stop("All samples will be removed.\n")  
  }
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) == nsamples(physeq)){
    cat("All samples will be preserved\n")
    res <- physeq
  }
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) < nsamples(physeq)){
    res <- prune_samples(samples = samples_to_keep, x = physeq)
  }
 
  return(res)
}

tax_glom_maslin_filtered_richness_vir1.2 <- phyloseq_richness_filter(tax_glom_fam_pruned_vir.2, mintaxa = 1)

#now at 1092 samples

meta_maaslin_vir1_melt.2 <- ps_melt(tax_glom_maslin_filtered_richness_vir1.2)

hmp2_vir_iphop_subset_vir1.2$Family

otu_to_family_vir1.2["C3030_k119_44190_flag=1_multi=6.7347_len=3738"] <- "Unknown"

maslin_meta_vir1.2$antibiotics <-
```

```{r}
#now also remove samples that have zero counts 



phyloseq_richness_filter <- function(physeq, mintaxa = 10){
  sp <- estimate_richness(physeq, measures = "Observed")
  samples_to_keep <- rownames(sp)[ which(sp$Observed >= mintaxa) ]
  
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) == 0){
    stop("All samples will be removed.\n")  
  }
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) == nsamples(physeq)){
    cat("All samples will be preserved\n")
    res <- physeq
  }
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) < nsamples(physeq)){
    res <- prune_samples(samples = samples_to_keep, x = physeq)
  }
 
  return(res)
}
```

```{r}
#sanity check - when i open and save the matrixcopy.csv - the file size is about half - making sure im not losing data by counting chatacters 

test1 <- read.csv("/Users/anshulsinha/Desktop/IBD_prophages_aim/bioinformatics_aim/bioinformatics_HMP_2/virome_data_08_2024/deseq/matrix.csv")
test2 <- read.csv("/Users/anshulsinha/Desktop/IBD_prophages_aim/bioinformatics_aim/bioinformatics_HMP_2/virome_data_08_2024/deseq/matrixcopy.csv")

clean_and_combine <- function(df) {
  # Convert the dataframe to a CSV-formatted string
  csv_string <- capture.output(write.csv(df, row.names = FALSE))
  
  # Combine lines into a single string
  combined_string <- paste(csv_string, collapse = "")
  
  # Remove backslashes, quotes, commas, and extra spaces
  cleaned_string <- gsub("[\\\",]", "", combined_string)  # Remove backslashes, quotes, commas
  cleaned_string <- gsub("\\s+", "", cleaned_string)       # Remove all whitespace (including spaces, tabs, newlines)
  
  return(cleaned_string)
}

num_count_df1 <- clean_and_combine(test1)
num_count_df2 <- clean_and_combine(test2)


char_count_df1 <- nchar(num_count_df1, type = "chars")
char_count_df2 <- nchar(num_count_df2, type = "chars")
char_count_df1-char_count_df2

#difference in 1,837 characters - probably due to the "V1" etc
```

```{r}
sample_mapping_hmp2 <- mutate_if(metadata_df, is.integer, as.factor)
```

```{r}
library(DESeq2)
#Use a simple paired design 
#basically treating that each sample has a non_dysbiotic and dysbiotic sample 


deseq2Data_hmp2 <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData=raw_counts_matrix_deseq2_hmp, colData=sample_mapping_hmp2_filtered, design = ~ Participant.ID + dysbiosis_binary)


```

```{r}
#DE results - not using a nested design like with Duerkop data, so no need to rebuild the model matrix here 
#need to first estimate size factors to deal with zeroes 

dds_hmp2 <-estimateSizeFactors(deseq2Data_hmp2, type = "poscounts")
dds_hmp2 <- DESeq(dds_hmp2)
```


```{r}

#significant values

keyvals_col_con_hmp2 <- ifelse(
    hmp_2_combined_deseq1_res2_df_changed$log2FoldChange < -1 & hmp_2_combined_deseq1_res2_df_changed$padj < 0.05, 'orange',
      ifelse(hmp_2_combined_deseq1_res2_df_changed$log2FoldChange > 1 & hmp_2_combined_deseq1_res2_df_changed$padj < 0.05, 'darkgreen',
        'light grey'))
  keyvals_col_con_hmp2[is.na(keyvals_col_con_hmp2)] <- 'light grey'
  names(keyvals_col_con_hmp2)[keyvals_col_con_hmp2 == 'darkgreen'] <- 'Enriched in Dysbiotic Samples'
  names(keyvals_col_con_hmp2)[keyvals_col_con_hmp2 == 'light grey'] <- 'Non-significant'
  names(keyvals_col_con_hmp2)[keyvals_col_con_hmp2 == 'orange'] <- 'Depleted in Dysbiotic Samples'

EV_col_con <- EnhancedVolcano(hmp_2_combined_deseq1_res2_df_changed, lab=NA , x = 'log2FoldChange',
    y = 'pvalue',  pCutoff = 0.05, cutoffLineWidth = 0.1, selectLab = rownames(hmp_2_combined_deseq1_res2_df_changed)[which(names(keyvals_col_con_hmp2) %in% c('Over-Abundant in Colitis', 'Over-Abundant in Control'))], pointSize = 2, gridlines.major = FALSE, gridlines.minor = FALSE, colCustom = keyvals_col_con_hmp2,  legendPosition = 'right', legendLabSize = 12,legendIconSize = 4.0, title = "", axisLabSize = 15,    subtitle = "", caption = "", xlab = expression(Log[2]~"Fold-Change")
)

EV_col_con


```

```{r}
edit_names_deseq <-  deseq2_results_hmp_2_comb[deseq2_results_hmp_2_comb$Family != "f__",]

             filter(padj<0.05)


 deseq.lfc.plot <-edit_names_deseq %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = log2FoldChange, reorder(Family, -log2FoldChange))) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0) +
  geom_point(aes(color = case_when(log2FoldChange > 1 & padj < 0.05 ~ "darkgreen", 
                                   log2FoldChange < -1 &  padj < 0.05 ~ "orange",
                                  TRUE ~ "grey" )), size = 5) +
  geom_point(data = . %>% filter(padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange > 1 | padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange < -1 ), size = 8, color = "darkred", shape = 1) +

  ) +

    breaks = c("darkgreen", "orange"),
    labels = c("Below 0", "Above 0")
  ) +
  guides(color = "none")  # Remove the legend

             filter(padj<0.05 & (log2FoldChange >= 1 | log2FoldChange <= -1))
```

```{r}
#Step 1 - Generate count tables for each of the following 
# family level 
#family level with temp or vir designation 

#1) tax glom- removing NAs - dont really want to include unknowns or single contigs here as they could influence DESEQ2 calc

#use the subset_1500

physeqhmp2whole_vir_i_reads.1500 <- prune_samples(sample.names.1500,  physeqhmp2whole_vir_i_reads)


 tax_glom_fam_reads.limma <- tax_glom(physeqhmp2whole_vir_i_reads.1500, taxrank="Family", NArm = TRUE) 
 
 

#2 include the same taxa that meet the prevalence cutoff 


####IMPORTANT### Filter out low prevlance PBFs - If I dont do this, DESEQ2 will artifically think that some of the low abdundance taxa are DE 
##Choosing arbitrarily that it must be found in at least 50 % of individuals per above prevalence analyses  - 

 #first melt the PS to determine the OTUs that correspond to the PBFs
ps_melted_filtered.limma <- ps_melt(tax_glom_fam_reads.limma)

length(unique(ps_melted_filtered.limma$OTU)) #74 left 

#now take the contignames that are associated with the families that I want to keep 
ps_melted_filtered.limma.prevalent.PBFs <- ps_melted_filtered.limma %>% 
  filter(Family %in% PBF_list_50_prev$Family )

length(unique(ps_melted_filtered.limma.prevalent.PBFs$Family)) #18 in here 

#now take the OTU names from this 
 keepTaxa = unique(ps_melted_filtered.limma.prevalent.PBFs$OTU)



tax_glom_fam_reads_limma.pruned <- prune_taxa(keepTaxa, tax_glom_fam_reads.limma)
 
###Went from 74 to 18 taxa 


#now also remove samples that have zero counts 



phyloseq_richness_filter <- function(physeq, mintaxa = 10){
  sp <- estimate_richness(physeq, measures = "Observed")
  samples_to_keep <- rownames(sp)[ which(sp$Observed >= mintaxa) ]
  
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) == 0){
    stop("All samples will be removed.\n")  
  }
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) == nsamples(physeq)){
    cat("All samples will be preserved\n")
    res <- physeq
  }
  
  if(length(samples_to_keep) < nsamples(physeq)){
    res <- prune_samples(samples = samples_to_keep, x = physeq)
  }
 
  return(res)
}

tax_glom_fam_reads_limma1 <- phyloseq_richness_filter(tax_glom_fam_reads_limma.pruned, mintaxa = 1)

#now at 1092 samples 





fam_reads_counts_limma <- psmelt(otu_table(tax_glom_fam_reads_limma1))


fam_reads_counts_limma.meta <- data.frame(filename = unique(fam_reads_counts_limma$Sample))
#sanity check - num individuals, diagnosis status, dysb vs. not 
fam_reads_counts_limma.meta <- left_join(fam_reads_counts_limma.meta, hmp2_metadata_phylo, by="filename")

length(unique(fam_reads_counts_limma.meta$Participant.ID))
table(fam_reads_counts_limma.meta$diagnosis.x)
table(fam_reads_counts_limma.meta$dysbiosis_binary)

#115 individuals 
#326 non-IBD samples, 492 CD, 274 UC 
#952 Non, 140 dysbiotic samples 



  
#3) PS melt to generate counts table as df 

fam_reads_countslimma1 <- psmelt(otu_table(tax_glom_fam_reads_limma1))

#For the two family names, it saves the agglomerated OTU name as a contig name (not the family name) - I'll have to change that here by left joining with a version of the iPHOP table (just family)

df_hmp_free_iphop_all_taxa_only_fam <- select(df_hmp_iphop_all, -c("Main.method","Confidence.score", "Additional.methods", "Virulent", "Temperate", "Lifestyle", "Host.genome", "Kingdom","Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Genus", "Species"))
colnames(df_hmp_free_iphop_all_taxa_only_fam) <- c("OTU", "Family")



#remove NAs 
df_hmp_free_iphop_all_taxa_only_fam  <- df_hmp_free_iphop_all_taxa_only_fam[!is.na(df_hmp_free_iphop_all_taxa_only_fam$Family),]


#Left Join
fam_reads_countslimma2 <- left_join(fam_reads_countslimma1,  df_hmp_free_iphop_all_taxa_only_fam, by = "OTU" )
unique(fam_reads_countslimma2$Family)



#Remove the first col with the contigname

fam_reads_countslimma2 <- fam_reads_countslimma2[-1]



#pivot wider 

fam_reads_countslimma3 <- fam_reads_countslimma2 %>%
                 pivot_wider(names_from = Sample, values_from = Abundance)

fam_reads_countslimma3 <- tibble::column_to_rownames(fam_reads_countslimma3, "Family")



#covert to matrix 

fam_reads_counts_matrix_limma <- as.matrix(fam_reads_countslimma3)








```

```{r}

library(limma)
library(edgeR)

#doesn't seem like there is an obvious way to do this:
# difficulty lies in that there are multiple replicates per individual - probably too much variation to use the DESEQ2 collapsereplicates()
# THe forums suggest to use duplicateCorrelation from LIMMA 


#####  LIMMA AT THE FAMILY LEVEL - WHOLE VIROME - NO LIFESTYLE INFO ##################################



#For basic setup of the LIMMA workflow, I followed this: https://ucdavis-bioinformatics-training.github.io/2022-April-GGI-DE-in-R/data_analysis/DE_Analysis_with_quizzes_fixed

#counts table 
rownames(fam_reads_counts_matrix_limma)





#metadata 
metadata_df_limma.1 <- hmp2_metadata_phylo

#filter for samples in matrix 

names.limma <- unique(fam_reads_countslimma2$Sample)
length(names.limma)


metadata_df_limma <- metadata_df_limma.1 %>% 
  filter(filename %in% names.limma)


#It’s very important to check that the samples are in the same order in the metadata and in the counts table, particularly since no errors will be generated if they aren’t–you’ll just get nonsense results.
rownames(metadata_df_limma) <- metadata_df_limma$filename

identical(rownames(metadata_df_limma), colnames(fam_reads_counts_matrix_limma))

#this is false ! to correct 
fam_reads_counts_matrix_1 <- fam_reads_counts_matrix_limma[,rownames(metadata_df_limma)]

#check again... now try 
identical(rownames(metadata_df_limma), colnames(fam_reads_counts_matrix_1))

#turn counts into a DGELIST 

d0_fam_counts <- DGEList(fam_reads_counts_matrix_1)

#now calculate normalization factors 
d0_fam_counts <- calcNormFactors(d0_fam_counts)





#Now we need to filter low expressed genes 
#To do that we will use the EdgeR filterby Expr 
#first we need to create a simple model -

mm_famcounts_diagnosis <- model.matrix(~0 + diagnosis.x, data = metadata_df_limma)
mm_famcounts_diagnosis


#Back to filtering

keep_famcounts_diagnosis <- filterByExpr(d0_fam_counts, mm_famcounts_diagnosis)
sum(keep_famcounts_diagnosis) # This keeps all 18




#filter using this 
d_fam_counts <- d0_fam_counts[keep_famcounts_diagnosis,]




# Voom transformation and calculation of variance weights


vobj_tmp_fam = voom( d_fam_counts, mm_famcounts_diagnosis, plot=TRUE)


#The mean variance trend does not look great, but I dont want to remove anymore samples, so I will rpoceed 

#First duplicate correlation 
dupcor_fam <- duplicateCorrelation(vobj_tmp_fam,mm_famcounts_diagnosis,block=metadata_df_limma$Participant.ID)



# run voom considering the duplicateCorrelation results
# in order to compute more accurate precision weights
# Otherwise, use the results from the first voom run
vobj_fam = voom( d_fam_counts, mm_famcounts_diagnosis, plot=TRUE, block=metadata_df_limma$Participant.ID, correlation=dupcor_fam$consensus)



# Estimate linear mixed model with a single variance component
# Fit the model for each gene, 
dupcor_fam2 <- duplicateCorrelation(vobj_fam, mm_famcounts_diagnosis, block=metadata_df_limma$Participant.ID)


# But this  uses  the virome-wide average for the random effect
fitDupCor_fam <- lmFit(vobj_fam, mm_famcounts_diagnosis, block=metadata_df_limma$Participant.ID, correlation=dupcor_fam2$consensus)

#making contrasts -followed this https://www.bioconductor.org/packages/devel/workflows/vignettes/RNAseq123/inst/doc/limmaWorkflow.html#creating-a-design-matrix-and-contrasts
contr.matrix_fam <- makeContrasts(
   CDvnonIBD = diagnosis.xCD - diagnosis.xnonIBD, 
   UCvnonIBD = diagnosis.xUC - diagnosis.xnonIBD, 
   CDvUC = diagnosis.xCD - diagnosis.xUC, 
   levels = colnames(mm_famcounts_diagnosis))

fitDupCor_fam <- contrasts.fit(fitDupCor_fam, contrasts=contr.matrix_fam)




# Fit Empirical Bayes for moderated t-statistics
fitDupCor_fam <- eBayes( fitDupCor_fam )



summary(decideTests(fitDupCor_fam))
fitDupCor_fam$coefficients

CD_nonIBD_fam <- topTable(fitDupCor_fam, coef = "CDvnonIBD", adjust="BH", number = 18)
UC_nonIBD_fam <- topTable(fitDupCor_fam, coef = "UCvnonIBD", adjust="BH", number = 18)
CD_UC_fam <- topTable(fitDupCor_fam, coef = "CDvUC", adjust="BH", number = 18)


CD_nonIBD_fam
UC_nonIBD_fam
CD_UC_fam


##ggplot2 plot the genes and their LFC


library(tidyverse)
# filter dataframe to get data to be highligheted
CD_nonIBD_fam_df_highlight <- CD_nonIBD_fam %>% 
             filter(adj.P.Val<0.05)







##VOLCANO PLOTS 







##ggplot2 plot the genes and their LFC
CD_nonIBD_fam_df <-  tibble::rownames_to_column(CD_nonIBD_fam, "Family")
#UC plot fam level

CD_nonIBD_fam_df$Family <- gsub("f__", "", CD_nonIBD_fam_df$Family)
           

#CD plot fam level

CD_nonIBD_fam_df %>%
 ggplot(aes(x = logFC, reorder(Family, -logFC))) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0) +
  geom_point(aes(color = case_when(logFC > 1 & adj.P.Val < 0.05 ~ "#800000", 
                                   logFC < -1 &  adj.P.Val < 0.05 ~ "#023020",
                                  TRUE ~ "grey" )), size = 5) +
  geom_point(data = . %>% filter(adj.P.Val < 0.05 & logFC > 1 | adj.P.Val < 0.05 & logFC < -1 ), size = 8, color = "darkred", shape = 1) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic", size = 10),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "italic")
```

```{r}
#AMG table 

#will need just the samples without 1500 
df_hmp_virome_merged.2 <- left_join(filtered.1500.df_hmp_virome, hmp2_vir_iphop, by="contigname")
df_hmp_virome_merged.2.iphop <- df_hmp_virome_merged.2[!is.na(df_hmp_virome_merged.2$Family),]

length(unique(df_hmp_virome_merged.2$contigname))
length(unique(df_hmp_virome_merged.2.iphop$contigname))


df_hmp_virome_merged.2.names <- unique(df_hmp_virome_merged.2$contigname)



#3886 contigs - need to filter to get the 3,870 

df_hmp_iphop_all.analyses.one <-   df_hmp_iphop_all_1

df_hmp_iphop_all.analyses <- df_hmp_iphop_all.analyses.one %>% 
  filter(contigname %!in% df_hmp_virome_merged.2.names)





AMGs <- read.table("/Users/anshulsinha/Desktop/IBD_prophages_aim/bioinformatics_aim/bioinformatics_HMP_2/virome_data_08_2024/AMGs/concatenated_AMGs.tsv", sep = "\t", quote = "", header = TRUE)

#create new column to indicate where the contig came from 


AMGs <- AMGs %>%
  mutate(participant.ID = sub("^[^_]*_[^_]*_[^_]*_([^\\.]+)\\..*$", "\\1", filename))

#now append to the contig name to be consistent with contig naming format 

AMGs$contigname <- paste(AMGs$participant.ID,"_",AMGs$scaffold, sep = "")

#replace whitespace with underscores for consistency 
AMGs$contigname <- gsub(" ", "_", AMGs$contigname)


#now filter so I only include the 3,870 contigs I care about - ones with iphop assignments 

length(unique(df_hmp_virome_merged.2.iphop$contigname)) #3,370

iphop.names <- df_hmp_virome_merged.2.iphop$contigname

#filter to just contain those names 
AMGs$contigname 

AMGs.filtered <- AMGs %>% 
  filter(contigname %in% iphop.names)

length(unique(AMGs.filtered$participant.ID)) 


length(unique(AMGs.filtered$protein)) 

#left with only 1,789 rows - suggests that not every contig has an AMG - sanity check to confirm that I am not missing anything 




'%!in%' <- function(x,y)!('%in%'(x,y))


AMGs.filtered.out <- AMGs %>% 
  filter(contigname %!in% iphop.names)


#read in C3001 VIBRANT file

df_hmp_iphop_all.analyses.one

df_hmp_iphop_all.analyses.filtered <- df_hmp_iphop_all.analyses

length(df_hmp_iphop_all.analyses.filtered$contigname) #3,870


#if "NA" change to contig name

3870-500  #3,370 have PBF membership
```
